![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/90MM_2022_42-scaled-e1776707468103.png)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/90MM_2022_42-scaled-e1776707468103-1024x380.png)

# What is the V/TO?

The V/TO™, or Vision/Traction Organizer™, is the two-page foundational tool in the [Entrepreneurial Operating System® (EOS®)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos/) that captures a company’s complete vision and plan on one piece of paper, front and back. It answers the eight questions every leadership team must agree on: [Core Values](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-values/), Core Focus™, 10-Year Target™, Marketing Strategy™, 3-Year Picture™, 1-Year Plan™, Quarterly [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks/), and [Issues List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-list/).

The V/TO gets the vision out of the leadership team’s head and onto paper, where it can be communicated, shared, and executed. It is the single most important document in a company running on EOS.

## Why the V/TO exists

Most entrepreneurs have a vision for their business. Very few can articulate it clearly, and almost none have it shared by their leadership team. The result is chaos disguised as activity.

The V/TO solves this in two pages. Page one is the Vision. Page two is the Traction. Together, they answer the eight questions that turn a hope into a plan.

When every leader on a team can recite the same Core Values, the same Core Focus, the same 10-Year Target, and the same quarterly Rocks, the company gains a superpower. Alignment.

## The two pages of the V/TO

### Page 1: Vision

Page one answers the long-range questions about where the company is going.

- Core Values
- Core Focus
- 10-Year Target
- Marketing Strategy

### Page 2: Traction

Page two answers the near-term questions about how the company will execute.

- 3-Year Picture
- 1-Year Plan
- Quarterly Rocks
- Issues List

## The eight questions of the V/TO

### Question 1: What are your Core Values?

Three to seven timeless guiding principles that define who you are and how you operate. Core Values are not aspirational. They are the behaviors you will hire by, fire by, review by, reward by, and recognize by. Core Values already exist in your company. The work is discovering them, not inventing them.

### Question 2: What is your Core Focus?

Your reason for being, made up of two parts: your Purpose, Cause, or Passion (why you exist) and your Niche (what you do best). The Core Focus is what your company should never stop doing. It is your sweet spot. Everything that pulls you away from it is a distraction.

### Question 3: What is your 10-Year Target?

A single, long-range goal that inspires the whole company. Big. Hairy. Audacious. The 10-Year Target gives everyone a direction to run toward. It is measurable, ambitious, and clear. Examples: “Reach $100M in revenue.” “Be the #1 provider in our region.” “Help 10,000 businesses run better.”

### Question 4: What is your Marketing Strategy?

Four elements:

- Your Target Market, also called “The List.” The demographic, geographic, and psychographic profile of your ideal customer.
- Your Three Uniques™. The three things that make your company different and better.
- Your Proven Process. The repeatable way you deliver value.
- Your Guarantee. The bold promise that reduces buyer risk.

### Question 5: What is your 3-Year Picture?

A vivid, detailed description of what the company looks like three years from now. Revenue. Profit. Headcount. What the company does. What clients experience. Who is on the team. Written in the present tense so the leadership team can see it.

### Question 6: What is your 1-Year Plan?

This year’s revenue, profit, and measurables. Plus the three to seven most important goals for the year. The 1-Year Plan is the first year of the 3-Year Picture. It must be specific and believable.

### Question 7: What are your Quarterly Rocks?

The three to seven most important priorities for the current 90-day quarter. These are the Rocks that, when completed, keep the company on track for the 1-Year Plan.

### Question 8: What are your Issues?

The running list of problems, obstacles, opportunities, and ideas the company must address. Long-term issues live on the V/TO Issues List. Short-term issues live on the [Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting/) Issues List.

## How to create a V/TO

The V/TO is built across two full days, typically called Vision Building Day 1 and Vision Building Day 2.

### Vision Building Day 1

Focus: page one of the V/TO.

- Core Values
- Core Focus
- 10-Year Target
- Marketing Strategy

Day 1 is about who you are and why you exist. Most leadership teams spend significant time in disagreement before reaching alignment. That is the point.

### Vision Building Day 2

Focus: page two of the V/TO.

- 3-Year Picture
- 1-Year Plan
- Quarterly Rocks
- Issues List

Day 2 translates the vision into an actionable plan with measurables, dates, and owners.

Most companies run Vision Building Days with a Professional EOS Implementer. The outside perspective is critical for pushing through the hard conversations.

## How to use the V/TO every quarter

The V/TO is a living document. Review it every quarter.

- At the Quarterly Pulsing Session, the leadership team reviews the V/TO, checks that the vision is still right, and sets the next quarter’s Rocks.
- At the Annual Planning session, the leadership team refreshes the 3-Year Picture and sets the new 1-Year Plan.
- Every State of the Company meeting, the full company hears the V/TO.

A V/TO that sits in a drawer is worthless. A V/TO that gets reviewed, updated, and communicated is priceless.

## Common V/TO mistakes

- Doing it alone. The V/TO works because the leadership team agrees on every answer. One person writing it in a vacuum defeats the tool.
- Too many Core Values. Three to seven is the range. Ten is too many. Nobody can remember ten.
- Vague Core Focus. “We help businesses grow” is not a Core Focus. It is a slogan.
- Weak 10-Year Target. If the 10-Year Target does not scare the leadership team a little, it is too small.
- Ignoring the Issues List. The bottom of page two is where the real work lives. A short Issues List means the team is not being honest.
- Writing it once and never updating it. The V/TO is a tool, not a framed poster.

## How the V/TO connects to the rest of EOS

The V/TO is the anchor for every other EOS tool:

- [Accountability Chart](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart/). The structure you need to execute the V/TO.
- [Scorecard](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard/). Measures weekly progress toward the 1-Year Plan.
- [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks/). Quarterly priorities from the V/TO.
- [Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting/). Weekly forum to keep the V/TO on track.
- [Core Values](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-values/). Used every day to hire, fire, and review people.

Everything in EOS serves the V/TO. Without a clear V/TO, the rest of the tools lack a target.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What does V/TO stand for?**

Vision/Traction Organizer. It is the two-page EOS tool that captures a company’s vision and plan.

**How long does it take to create a V/TO?**

Two full days, typically called Vision Building Day 1 and Vision Building Day 2.

**Is the V/TO trademarked?**

Yes. V/TO and Vision/Traction Organizer are trademarks of EOS Worldwide.

**Who should be involved in creating the V/TO?**

The full leadership team. Typically five to seven people. Everyone on the top level of the Accountability Chart.

**How often should the V/TO be updated?**

Every quarter. Rocks change every 90 days. The 1-Year Plan refreshes annually. The 3-Year Picture refreshes annually. Core Values, Core Focus, and 10-Year Target change rarely.

**Can I create a V/TO without an EOS Implementer?**

Yes. The V/TO template is freely available through EOS Academy. Many companies self-implement. Working with a Professional EOS Implementer accelerates the process and creates accountability.

## Related EOS Tools

- [Core Values](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-values/)
- [Core Focus](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-focus/)
- [10-Year Target](https://www.eosworldwide.com/10-year-target/)
- [Marketing Strategy](https://www.eosworldwide.com/marketing-strategy/)
- [3-Year Picture](https://www.eosworldwide.com/3-year-picture/)
- [1-Year Plan](https://www.eosworldwide.com/1-year-plan/)
- [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks/)
- [Accountability Chart](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart/)
- [Scorecard](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard/)

## Start Building Your V/TO

Ready to get your vision out of your head and onto paper? [Start with a Free 90-Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started) with a Professional EOS Implementer.

**Written by**EOS Worldwide

**Reviewed by**[Mark O'Donnell](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-history-team), Visionary & CEO, EOS Worldwide

EOS Worldwide is the organization behind the [Entrepreneurial Operating System®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos/). Content reflects official EOS® doctrine.

[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks#content "Skip to content")

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/pexels-pixabay-235990-scaled.jpg)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/pexels-pixabay-235990-1024x683.jpg)

# What Are [EOS](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos/) Rocks™?

Rocks are the three to seven most important priorities that a company, department, or individual must complete in the next 90 days. Rocks create a 90-Day World. They turn a one-year plan into focused, bite-size chunks that a team can actually execute. Each Rock is specific, measurable, and attainable, with a single owner and a clear due date.

Rocks are one of the most powerful tools in EOS. They solve the universal problem of trying to do everything at once and getting nothing done.

## Why Rocks exist

Most organizations start the year with a long list of priorities and finish the year having accomplished almost none of them. The problem is not effort. The problem is focus.

Rocks fix this by forcing a choice. Three to seven priorities per quarter. No more. The team commits. The team executes. The team measures at 90 days.

The name comes from an analogy popularized by Stephen Covey in First Things First, then adapted by Verne Harnish. Picture a glass cylinder. Next to it, you have rocks, gravel, sand, and water. The cylinder is your time. The rocks are your true priorities. The gravel is your day-to-day work. The sand is the interruptions. The water is everything else.

If you pour in the water, sand, and gravel first, the rocks never fit.

If you put the rocks in first, everything else fills in around them and it all fits.

Rocks go in first.

## What makes a good Rock

A Rock must be specific, measurable, and attainable. Ambiguity kills Rocks.

Good Rocks:

- Close $1 million in new business
- Hire a new Controller
- Document the customer onboarding process and train all customer success reps
- Narrow CFO candidates to two finalists
- Launch the new website

Bad Rocks:

- Improve sales
- Work on culture
- Start thinking about hiring
- Get the team aligned

The test is simple. At the end of the quarter, can anyone in the room say without debate whether the Rock is done?

Yes means it is a good Rock. Ambiguity means it is not.

## How to set Rocks: the 8-step process

### Step 1: Brainstorm

After reviewing your [V/TO™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto/), the leadership team lists everything on a whiteboard that needs to be accomplished in the next 90 days. You will typically land on 10 to 20 items. One client once came up with 75.

### Step 2: Narrow the list

Debate and discuss. Make a decision on each one: keep it, kill it, or combine it. Keep narrowing until you have the three to seven most important priorities for the company this quarter. The right ones rise to the top.

### Step 3: Set the due date and define each Rock

Set the due date (typically end of the quarter: March 31, June 30, September 30, December 31). Then define each Rock clearly. Specific, measurable, attainable.

### Step 4: Assign a single owner

Each Rock must be owned by one person. When more than one person is accountable, nobody is accountable. The owner drives the Rock to completion: creates the timeline, calls the meetings, pushes the work forward.

### Step 5: Individual Rocks

Once company Rocks are set, each leadership team member sets their own Rocks. First, they carry forward any company Rocks they own. Then they add their most important three to seven individual Rocks.

### Step 6: Create the Rock Sheet

Create a one-page landscape Rock Sheet. Company Rocks at the top. Individual Rocks below. Bring it to every Level 10 Meeting™. This sheet drives the weekly Rock Review.

### Step 7: Share with the organization

Share the company Rocks with every employee in the quarterly State of the Company meeting. The vision must be shared by all. Everyone deserves to know what matters this quarter.

### Step 8: Cascade to departments

Each department sets their Rocks using the same process. Every employee ends up with their own Rocks for the quarter. Leadership team members should have three to seven Rocks. Everyone else should have one to three.

## How many Rocks should a company have?

Three to seven. Closer to three is better.

The temptation is always to do more. Resist it. When everything is important, nothing is important. Less is always better. A few priorities executed brilliantly beats a long list executed poorly.

For individual employees outside the leadership team, the cap is three. Leadership teams can stretch to seven. Department-level Rocks should rarely exceed five.

## Why Rocks are 90 days

Ninety days is the maximum human attention span for a priority. Longer than that, and focus drifts. The work slides. Quarters are also the natural rhythm of business: annual goals break cleanly into four quarters, and each quarter creates natural energy and accountability.

The 90-Day World also builds in a safety net. If you set the wrong Rocks, you only lose one quarter before you correct course. If you set the right ones, you compound four times per year.

## Tracking Rocks in a Level 10 Meeting

Every Level 10 Meeting includes a 5-minute Rock Review. Each owner reports on-track or off-track. No discussion during the review. If a Rock is off-track, it drops to the [Issues List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-list/) for IDS.

On-track means the owner is confident they will complete the Rock by the end of the quarter. Off-track means they are not.

Do not let owners fudge this. An honest off-track is more valuable than a dishonest on-track.

## Common Rock mistakes

- Garbage in, garbage out. If you set the wrong Rocks, you spend a whole quarter pointed in the wrong direction. Spend the time to set them right.
- Too many Rocks. More than seven for leadership, more than three for individuals, is a recipe for nothing getting done.
- Vague Rocks. “Improve the onboarding experience” is not a Rock. “Launch new onboarding workflow and train all 12 reps by March 31” is.
- Shared ownership. Two people owning a Rock means no one owns it. One owner per Rock.
- Adding Rocks mid-quarter. Once the quarter starts, no new Rocks. New ideas go on the V/TO Issues List for next quarter.
- Commitment fizzle. Starting strong and fading by week four. Rocks need discipline every week, not just every quarter.

## How Rocks connect to the rest of EOS

Rocks sit inside the [Meeting Pulse](https://www.eosworldwide.com/meeting-pulse/). Annual planning sets the yearly goals. Quarterly Pulsing sets the Rocks. The Weekly Level 10 Meeting tracks them. Every 90 days, the cycle repeats.

Rocks also connect to the V/TO. The Issues List at the bottom of the V/TO is where next quarter’s Rock candidates often come from. The [1-Year Plan](https://www.eosworldwide.com/1-year-plan/) breaks into Rocks. The Rocks break into To-Dos.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is the difference between a Rock and a to-do?**

A Rock is a 90-day priority. A To-Do is a seven-day action item. Rocks drive the quarter. To-Dos drive the week.

**How many Rocks should each person have?**

Leadership team members: three to seven. Department managers: three to five. Individual employees: one to three.

**Can a Rock be longer than 90 days?**

No. The 90-day container is intentional. If the work takes longer, break it into multiple Rocks across multiple quarters.

**What happens if a Rock is not completed?**

Carry it forward to the next quarter only if it is still a top priority. Otherwise, let it go. A Rock that carries for three quarters was never a real priority.

**Is “Rock” the same as a SMART goal?**

Rocks are SMART goals applied to a 90-day window with a single owner. They share the specific, measurable, and attainable principles.

**Is EOS Rocks trademarked?**

Yes. The EOS Rocks methodology and related terms are trademarks of EOS Worldwide.

## Related EOS Tools

- [V/TO](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto/). Where Rocks come from.
- [Scorecard™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard/). Measures weekly progress on what matters most.
- [Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting/). Where Rocks get reviewed every week.
- [Meeting Pulse](https://www.eosworldwide.com/meeting-pulse/). The quarterly Pulsing Session is where Rocks get set.
- [Issues List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-list/). Where Rock candidates often originate.

## Start Setting Rocks That Get Done

Ready to turn your priorities into progress? [Start with a Free 90-Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started) with a Professional EOS Implementer.

**Written by**EOS Worldwide

**Reviewed by**[Mark O'Donnell](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-history-team), Visionary & CEO, EOS Worldwide

EOS Worldwide is the organization behind the [Entrepreneurial Operating System®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos/). Content reflects official EOS® doctrine.

[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-level-10-meeting#content "Skip to content")

- [EOS Mastery](https://www.eosworldwide.com/category/blog)

# Level 10 Meeting™

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/image-32-96x96.png)

- Mark O'Donnell

Anyone reading this has probably sat through countless meetings that could’ve been an email. People go on tangents, the organizer lacks any real agenda, and people walk away wondering why the heck they went. It can feel like death by 1,000 hours of pointless meetings. Enter the [Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting).

## What’s a Level 10 Meeting?

As part of the [Entrepreneurial Operating System® (EOS®)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos), the Level 10 Meeting (L10) creates a standard structure for productive meetings. No, really. EOS lays out a template for team meetings to focus on the most important things each week without the nonsense.

On a scale of 1 to 10, most people would rank their current meetings at a 4 or 5. In some cases, even that’s being generous. In a Level 10 Meeting, attendees focus on the most important topics to spot issues before they become bigger problems. But that doesn’t come naturally (as evidenced by the prevalence of crappy meetings). To get there, companies Running on EOS™ use the standardized L10 agenda.

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/EOS-blog-CTA-Bannerstool-4-scaled.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-level-10-meeting-download)

## What Makes a Level 10 Meeting So Effective?

Because Level 10 meetings operate at a repeatable, strategic level, they provide a solid framework covering material that matters. Opinions and politicking have no place in a meeting that focuses strictly on objectives and metrics. Teams also learn to minimize tangents and sidebar conversations to cover only what matters.

In addition, the same two individuals (or their proxies during vacations) serve as the facilitator and the scribe for every meeting. This removes the question, “Who’s leading this meeting?” The same goes for the scribe role: Everyone already knows who’s taking notes. Removing this ambiguity saves precious minutes discussing and agreeing to roles in each meeting.

## How Many People Should Attend an L10 Meeting?

While opinions vary, generally, six or seven appears to be the ideal number for optimal Level 10 Meeting effectiveness. Fewer than four may not result in meaningful dialogue. More than seven become a crowd, making it difficult to hold discussions or add value for everyone attending.

The facilitator should look for context clues to gauge whether an L10 has the optimal number of attendees. Does everyone seem engaged in the discussion, or do some people look like they’ve checked out? Does the [Issues List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-list) carry more and more items week to week? This can signal too much discussion or too many people attending and adding new issues.

## The Level 10 Meeting Agenda

The L10 meeting agenda keeps a team focused and moving through a consistent process based on human nature. Developed through real-world testing, it serves as the most effective and efficient way to get a handle on a business. The team can get more done in just 90 minutes a week than in dozens of one-off meetings.

Teams should hold L10 meetings at the **same time** on the **same day each week**. Most importantly, they need to start on time and end on time. Attendance is mandatory, with the exception of vacation or death.

In addition, every L10 meeting has both a facilitator and a scribe. The facilitator moves the team through the agenda to keep on schedule. The scribe records updates, takeaways, and any items for the To-Do List or Issues List.

The agenda consists of the following sections:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Section** | **Time allotted** |
| Segue | 5 minutes |
| [Scorecard](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard) Review | 5 minutes |
| [Rock](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks) Review | 5 minutes |
| Customer / Employee Headlines | 5 minutes |
| To-Do Lists | 5 minutes |
| [Identify, Discuss, Solve (IDS™)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/ids) | 60 minutes |
| Conclude | 5 minutes |

We cover the specifics of each section below.

Level 10 Meeting® - YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Level 10 Meeting®](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1_iAxgB5k-k) [EOS Worldwide](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzV6bD_MPmZLJo7QmP9yqDw)

EOS Worldwide35.1K subscribers

[Watch on](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1_iAxgB5k-k)

### The Segue

As people gather for the weekly meeting, [the segue](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/love-level-10-meeting-segue) creates the time to disconnect mentally from other distractions. To start the meeting on time, everyone should arrive early and be ready to go at the appointed start time.

No matter what happened just before the meeting, the 5-minute segue allows everyone to refocus. Each team member shares good news (both personal and professional) from the previous week. This helps remind the team that they work with real-life humans with other responsibilities and outside passions.

The quick cadence of these weekly “bests” keeps the agenda moving into the next section: the EOS Scorecard™ review.

### Scorecard Review

During this 5-minute report, the team reviews the 5–15 most important metrics to track. The [Scorecard review](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/measured-managed) provides objective proof of whether the team is hitting its goals and helps identify issues early.

Each number’s owner reports whether their specific item is “on track” or “off track.”

Most importantly, team members should provide only one of these two responses, resisting the temptation to launch into any explanations.

Any off-track numbers or items that require additional discussion should drop to the Issues section. By the way, “drop” means to add the item to the Issues List for discussion later in the meeting.

Again, this quick cadence moves the team to the next measurable section: the quarterly Rock review.

### Rock Review

Every team member has accountability for completing approximately 3–5 quarterly goals (or [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/identifying-quarterly-rocks)). These quarterly Rocks represent the most important actions to achieve in the next 90 days for the team’s greater good. Each Rock can have only _one_ owner.

To keep the discussion to a minimum, this 5-minute section again uses “on track” or “off track” responses from the Rock owner. Any questions, explanations, or further discussions belong in the Issues section.

Team members now have a good understanding of their current standings based on the Scorecard and Rock reviews. Time to move on to the next section to share Headlines.

### Headlines

This brief section shares any employee or customer news, good or bad. The facilitator will ask each member for any [headlines](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/high-impact-low-cost-recognition) to share with the team. This allows the team to celebrate good news and potentially drop any bad news for later discussion.

Expect to spend 5 minutes max on this section before heading to the To-Do List.

### To-Do List

While Rocks represent 90-day priorities, the [To-Do List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/overwhelmed-with-too-many-to-dos) captures action items to complete within 7 days. They represent actions that typically a team wouldn’t capture. But once on the To-Do List, the team holds the responsible owner accountable for completing it.

Here, To-Dos fall under either “done” or “not done.” No conversation, no explanations, and certainly no whining. Any not-done items can carry to the following week or drop down to the Issues List.

This exercise should take around 5 minutes to complete. Then the team can get to the actual meat of the meeting: Identify, Discuss, and Solve (IDS) all those issues you’ve recorded!

### IDS

Welcome to the magic (and the secret to the success of L10 meetings)!

Here the person raising the issue will clearly **Identify** it. Then the team **Discusses** the issue until they get to its root cause. After every detail has been laid out, the team will determine the best way to **Solve** the issue forever.

This meeting section will take the longest – around an hour if you’ve timed the other sections correctly. During IDS, the team discusses all the [issues](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/issue-solution) raised earlier. Typically, the Issues List contains 5–15 items. But sometimes, a few will carry over from the previous week.

With all these issues listed out, the team will rank the three most important issues to tackle right away. They start with the top issue, working to understand its root cause. From there, they focus solely on solving the issue. Once it’s solved, the team goes on to the next issue.

They continue to identify the next three most important issues to solve until 5 minutes remains in the meeting. Regardless of how many issues remain on the list, the facilitator moves to conclude the meeting.

### Conclude

This vital section pulls the whole meeting together, ensuring no loose ends need addressing. In these last 5 minutes, the facilitator quickly reviews all new items on the To-Do List and any resolutions that require additional communication outside the meeting.

Finally, each team member ranks the effectiveness of the meeting before the facilitator ends the meeting on time. Every time.

## Level 10 Meeting™ Template

By repeating this agenda, you create the foundation for your company to finally have productive meetings. It may take a while to get everyone used to the pace of the shorter sections and hold their comments for IDS. But keep at it! When your team starts solving an average of 3–5 Issues per meeting, they solve them forever. You’ll often find that other items on the Issues List disappear because they were symptoms of the root cause.

**Ready to get started?** Our [EOS Implementers](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-an-eos-implementer) provide Level 10 Meeting™ business consulting and educational services to entrepreneurial businesses across the world to help them conduct the most effective meetings possible. [Get matched with an EOS Implementer today!](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started)

**Are you a business coach or entrepreneurial leader ready to guide others?** The Level 10 Meeting Agenda isn’t just a tool—it’s part of a proven framework for business growth. Join the ranks of EOS Implementers and use EOS tools to spark clarity, accountability, and meaningful transformation. [Learn how to become an EOS Implementer now.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-webinar-registration)

# BECOME A PROFESSIONALEOS IMPLEMENTER.

As an EOS Implementer franchise, you'll guide leadership teams to new heights—doing what you love, making a difference, and finally living life on your terms.

[Watch the Webinar →](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-webinar-registration) [Already watched? Book a discovery call →](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-franchise-application)

## Grow Great Companies. Build a Great Life.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/gallery-desktop-1024x414.png)

#### **A Blueprint for the Life You Want**

Do what you love, with people you love, while making a huge difference, being paid well, and creating time for what matters most.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/check_5-1.png)

#### **Ongoing Training & Career Growth**

From Base Camp to advanced workshops, you’ll continually sharpen your skills and grow your impact.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/check_5-1.png)

#### **The $400K+ System**

Designed to help get you to $400K with 20 Clients as an EOS Implementer, with expert support at every step.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/check_5-1.png)

#### **Client Growth Without Cold Outreach**

Connect with high-intent prospects through the EOS Leads Program. No cold calling, no chasing. Just qualified introductions.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/check_5-1.png)

#### **Grow Your Business by Growing Theirs**

Grow your coaching business by helping leaders scale theirs—using a simple, proven system that works.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/check_5-1.png)

#### **A Powerful Peer Network**

Join a high-caliber community of experienced EOS Implementers who share referrals, insights, and support.

Download the Franchise Guide

## Why Become an EOS Implementer?

EOS is a proven framework of simple tools that helps businesses achieve their goals. As an EOS Implementer, you coach and facilitate leadership teams using a structured system to drive results—without burnout. You’ll join a network of 1,000+ coaches and build a scalable, fulfilling practice.

_This short questionnaire helps you assess whether the EOS Implementer path fits how you work, how you lead, and what you want next._

[Take the 1-Minute Quiz →](https://academy.eosworldwide.com/eosi-questionnaire)

What You Get

With EOS, you work for yourself—but never alone. Gain everything you need to build, grow, and scale your coaching business.

- **Proven coaching framework** to drive results
- **Marketing & sales tools** to promote your business
- **Ongoing training & development**
- **Client recruitment support** to build your roster
- **High earning potential** & independent schedule
- **A community & network** of like-minded professionals

What You Gain

EOS isn’t just about building a business—it’s about creating a life you love while making an impact. Discover the EOS Life for yourself.

- Doing what you love
- With people you love
- Making a huge difference
- Being compensated appropriately
- With time for other passions

## The Path to Launching Your Coaching Business

STEP THREE

STEP FOUR

Training &

Launch

data.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/why-eos#content "Skip to content")

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/default-featured-image.png)

# Get Everything You Want from Your Business

Running a growing business should be exciting. EOS helps leadership teams keep it that way.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/header-gfx-why-eos-1024x939.png)

## These are the five frustrations that hold most teams back.

### Most leadership teams run into them—and growth only makes them worse.

People Problems  Lack of Control  Profit  Hitting the Ceiling  Nothing's Working

“We argued about the same issue 19 times.”

#### People Problems

### You’ve hired good people who care. Yet too much still rests on your shoulders.

You’re stepping into conversations that shouldn’t need you. Clarifying expectations that should already be clear.

When something slips, it’s hard to tell whether the issue is communication, capability, or ownership. You don’t want to hover or chase.

You want a team that knows their role, owns it fully, and executes without constant supervision.

EOS installs clarity and accountability across your leadership team, so ownership becomes real and sustainable.

“He was relaxed and successful. I wasn’t.”

#### Lack of Control

### You started this business for freedom, but now your days feel reactive.

You’re answering questions you thought others could handle. You’re pulled into issues that shouldn’t involve you. Even when things seem stable, it still feels fragile.

You don’t feel in control of your time. You feel responsible for everything.

EOS brings structure and discipline to your business, so it runs on a system, not on you.

"We increased revenues by 68% and margins by 41%.”

#### Profit Is Stagnant or Unpredictable

### You’re working hard and revenue may even be growing. But when you look at the bottom line, it doesn’t feel as steady as it should.

Some quarters feel strong and others feel tight. You find yourself wondering whether margins will hold, whether cash flow will line up, and whether the numbers will land where you need them to.

You didn’t take this risk to hope the math works out. You want predictable, consistent profitability.

EOS strengthens visibility and accountability, so your results become measurable and predictable.

"We grew from 150 to 500 employees."

#### Hitting the Ceiling

### Growth used to feel natural. Now it feels harder.

You push for progress, but something always seems to slow it down—a bottleneck, a people issue, a breakdown in execution. You solve one constraint, and another shows up. You can feel there's another level for your business, but you're not breaking through to it.

It's not a lack of effort. It's something structural.

EOS identifies and removes structural constraints, so growth becomes intentional and scalable.

“There’s a method to the madness.”

#### Nothing’s Working

### You’ve read books, rolled out initiatives, tried new systems, hired new people.

The activity is there—and for a while, things improve. But slowly and consistently, it fades. Old habits return. Discipline slips. And you’re left wondering why nothing seems to stick.

You don’t need another idea. You need a system that lasts.

EOS gives you a simple, complete operating system, so progress lasts.

## The Way Out: Vision. Traction. Healthy.

## When businesses get these three things right, the frustrations fade, and your business becomes simpler to run, easier to grow, and more enjoyable to lead.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-vision.svg)

### Vision

Your leaders get 100% on the same page about:

Where you're going

How you'll get there

What matters most right now

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-traction.svg)

#### Traction

You install real discipline and accountability.

The right people

Focused on the right priorities

Executing every week

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-healthy.svg)

#### Healthy

You build a cohesive, functional leadership team.

Issues get surfaced

Problems get solved

Meetings become productive

## When the Frustrations Disappear:

- You're back in control.
- Your team owns their seats.
- Issues get solved for good.
- Profit becomes predictable.
- Growth becomes intentional.
- You enjoy leading again.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx1-1024x688.jpg)

## Real Businesses. Real Results.

These leaders once faced the same frustrations. Here's how running on EOS changed their businesses—and can change yours.

"He was relaxed and successful. I wasn't."

##### Scott Seefeld

Titus Talent Strategies

"We argued about the same issue 19 times."

##### Anthony Karls

Rock Clicks

"We increased revenues by 68% and margins by 41%."

##### Charles Thayer

All-Around

"I didn't go to business school."

##### Makara Rumley

Hummingbird

"We grew from 150 to 500 employees."

##### Chris Thorkelson

Lloyd Companies

"There's a method to the madness."

##### Eric Sheerin

Dogwood Wealth Management

[More EOS Stories](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLw37fEddiEoFiuvCUS06MNriEqEJDx3RK)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-logo.svg)

## Frequently   Asked Questions

How do I know if EOS is right for my leadership team?

EOS is designed for privately held entrepreneurial companies with 10 to 250 employees whose leadership teams are open-minded, growth-oriented, and willing to be honest and vulnerable. If you're experiencing one or more of the Five Frustrations — People, Lack of Control, Profit, Hitting the Ceiling, or Nothing's Working — EOS is worth exploring. If you want real results and are willing to follow a simple, proven system with discipline, EOS is likely a fit.

Do we need to be in crisis to use EOS?

No. EOS is not a turnaround tool — it's an operating system for growth. Most companies that implement EOS are doing reasonably well but sense they could be doing significantly better. The most common trigger is hitting a ceiling: the business is growing but leadership feels like it's outpacing their ability to manage it.

What kind of results should we expect?

Clearer vision, faster decision-making, higher accountability, better team health, and measurable improvement in revenue and profit. Most teams see real change within 90 days. Full transformation typically takes 18 to 24 months. Success depends on the leadership team's commitment to doing the work.

Is EOS too simple for a complex business?

No. EOS is simple by design — simplicity creates clarity and scalability. The tools are not simplistic; they are distilled. EOS has been implemented in virtually every industry, including complex manufacturing, professional services, healthcare, technology, and financial services. Complexity does not disqualify a company from EOS. Unwillingness to commit does.

Can we implement EOS without hiring an EOS Implementer?

Yes. Many companies self-implement using _Traction_, the free tools at eosworldwide.com/tools, and the [EOS Academy](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-academy) self-serve resources. A Professional EOS Implementer accelerates the work — most Implementer-led implementations reach full traction in 18 to 24 months.

What is the first step to getting started with EOS?

Read _[Traction](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-book)_ by Gino Wickman. It takes about six hours. Then either take the free [Organizational Checkup](https://www.eosworldwide.com/organizational-checkup) at eosworldwide.com or [schedule a free 90-Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started) with a Professional EOS Implementer.

## Ready to Eliminate the Frustration for Good?

Thousands of leadership teams run on EOS. Make the decision to join them.

[Schedule a 90 Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started)

[Explore the Tools](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-tools)

[Take the Free Organizational Checkup](https://organizationalcheckup.com/)

# The Traction Library®

### EOS Books for Everyone   in Your Company

#### Every person on your team must have the right information and tools to implement the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS) purely throughout your organization. With the books in the Traction Library®, your entire company – from leadership to management to employees – will understand their role and be better equipped to help your company succeed.

[Save 50% when you order 20+ books](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-library-bulk-order)

[Order Now](https://a.co/d/0aVWOWbf) [Download Sample](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-book-free-chapter)

For EVERYONE

### **[Traction: Get a Grip on Your Business](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-book)**

#### by Gino Wickman

Inside **_Traction®_**, you’ll learn the secrets of strengthening the Six Key Components™ of your business. You’ll discover simple yet powerful ways to run your company that will give you and your leadership team more focus, more growth, and more enjoyment.

Based on years of real-world implementation in thousands of companies, the Entrepreneurial Operating System is a practical method for achieving the business success you have always envisioned.

[Audiobook Diagrams](https://www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/EOSWW.Traction.Diagrams.For_.Audiobook.Updated-1.pdf%20aria-label=Audiobook%20Diagrams)

For Visionaries & Integrators

### **[Rocket Fuel: The One Essential Combination](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocket-fuel-book)**

#### by Gino Wickman & Mark C. Winters

**_Rocket Fuel_** details the integral roles of the Visionary and Integrator and explains how an effective relationship between the two can thrive. You’ll also get advice to help Visionary-minded and Integrator-minded individuals find one another. When these two people come together to share their natural talents and innate skill sets, they have the power to reach new heights for virtually any company or organization.

Filled with familiar situations, real-world characters and detailed, fully-applied tools, **_Get A Grip_** vividly illustrates the right way to implement EOS® in an enjoyable fable format, making it easy for you to translate this story into results for your own business.

### **[How to Be a Great Boss](https://www.eosworldwide.com/how-to-be-a-great-boss-book)**

#### by Gino Wickman and René Boer

Studies have repeatedly shown that the majority of employees are disengaged at work—but it doesn’t have to be this way. Often, the difference between a group of indifferent employees and a fully engaged team comes down to one simple thing—a great boss. **_How to Be a Great Boss_** presents a straightforward, practical approach to help bosses at all levels of an organization get the most from their people.

### [**The EOS Life**](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-life-book)

#### by Gino Wickman

EOS Founder Gino Wickman describes the concept of the EOS Life®, which is doing what you love, with people you love, making a huge difference, being compensated appropriately, and with time for other passions. The EOS Life will show you everything you need to live your ideal life, a life that is absolutely achievable and 100% customizable uniquely for you.

The companion EOS Life Journal + Planner provides daily exercises, prompts, and planning pages to get you on the road to your EOS Life.

data.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-model#content "Skip to content")

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/default-featured-image.png)

# The EOS MODEL®

#### The EOS Model provides a visual illustration of the Six Key Components™ of any business that must be managed and strengthened to be a great business. This model applies to big and small businesses alike, in any industry.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/EOS-Model-1024x1024.png)

## The Six Key Components of Any Business

[Vision](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vision-component)

### [Vision](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vision-component)

Strengthening this component means getting everyone in the organization 100% on the same page with where you’re going and how you’re going to get there.

[People](https://www.eosworldwide.com/people-component)

### [People](https://www.eosworldwide.com/people-component)

Simply put, we can’t do it without great people. This means surrounding yourself with great people, top to bottom, because you can’t achieve a great vision without a great team.

[Data](https://www.eosworldwide.com/data-component)

### [Data](https://www.eosworldwide.com/data-component)

This means cutting through all the feelings, personalities, opinions, and egos and boiling your organization down to a handful of objective numbers that give you an absolute pulse on where things are.

#### With the Vision, People, and Data Components strong, you start to create a lucid, transparent, open and honest organization where everything becomes more visible and you start to “smoke out all the issues,” which leads to…

[Issues](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-component)

### [Issues](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-component)

Strengthening this component means becoming great at solving problems throughout the organization – setting them up, knocking them down, and making them go away forever.

[Process](https://www.eosworldwide.com/process-component)

### [Process](https://www.eosworldwide.com/process-component)

This is the secret ingredient in your organization. This means “systemizing” your business by identifying and documenting the core processes that define the way to run your business. You'll need to get everyone on the same page with what the essential procedural steps are, and then get everyone to follow them to create consistency and scalability in your organization.

[Traction<sup>®</sup>](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-component)

### [Traction®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-component)

This means bringing discipline and accountability into the organization – becoming great at execution – taking the vision down to the ground and making it real.

##### EOS ONE®.

## ONE VISION. ONE SYSTEM. ONE TEAM.™

Begin your 30-day free trial of the simple-to-use, all-in-one software for getting more of what you want from your business.

_Exclusively from the makers of EOS._

[TRY EOS ONE®. FOR FREE](https://www.eosone.com/)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/EOSoneDashboardImage.png)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-logo.svg)

## Frequently   Asked Questions

What is the EOS Model?

The EOS Model® is the visual representation of the Six Key Components™ of any business: Vision, People, Data, Issues, Process, and Traction. Strengthening all six drives a company into the top 5% of performers.

What are the Six Key Components of EOS?

Vision, People, Data, Issues, Process, and Traction. These are the six areas every company must strengthen, and [EOS®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos/) provides the tools to strengthen each one. Each Component now has a dedicated deep-dive book in the EOS Mastery Series.

What is the goal of EOS?

The goal of EOS is to help entrepreneurs get what they want from their businesses. That means three things: getting 100% on the same page about vision, gaining traction through disciplined execution, and building a healthy, cohesive leadership team.

What tools does EOS use to strengthen the Six Key Components?

The EOS Toolbox® contains the complete set of EOS tools — including the [V/TO®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto/), [Accountability Chart™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart/), [Scorecard](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard/), [Level 10 Meeting™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting/), [People Analyzer®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/people-analyzer/), [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks/), and [IDS®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/ids/). Each tool strengthens one or more of the Six Key Components.

What does it mean to strengthen a Key Component?

EOS measures strength in each Component on a scale of 0–100%. The goal is to get every Component to at least 80% strong. When all six are strong, symptomatic issues fade away and the company operates in the top 5% of performers.

How do I know how strong my company is across the Six Key Components?

Take the free [Organizational Checkup](https://www.eosworldwide.com/organizational-checkup). It is a 20-question self-assessment that scores your company across the Six Key Components. Most companies start at 20–40% and aim for 80% or higher.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/quote.svg)

To the degree you focus on strengthening these Six Components as leaders and managers, everything will fall into place. That will move your business into the top 5 percent.

Gino WickmanFounder of EOS

## How strong is your organization?

#### Understand your performance in each of the Six Key Components of your business.

[Take the Organizational Checkup](https://organizationalcheckup.com/)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/06/EOS_OrgCheckup_screens_mockup_2-1024x768.jpg)

## Learn More About EOS®

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/eos-process-partial.png)

### The EOS Process®

### The EOS Process®

Follow this proven process to purely implement the EOS Tools and get the results you want.

[Learn the Process](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-process)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/eos-tools-v2.png)

### The EOS Toolbox™

### The EOS Toolbox™

Use this complete set of practical tools to strengthen the Six Key Components of your business.

[Get Free Tools](https://www.eosworldwide.com/tools)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/get-started.png)

### Get Started

### Get Started

Ready to take the next step? Learn more about implementing EOS in your organization.

[Learn More](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/default-featured-image.png)

# YOU'RE ONE STEP AWAY FROM RUNNING A BETTER BUSINESS

Every EOS journey starts the same way: a free 90-Minute Meeting with an EOS Implementer. No obligation. No sales pitch. Just a clear look at where your business is and where it could go.

[Book My 90-Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/start-here#book-now)

###### Or explore on your own

[Browse the Implementer Directory](http://implementer.eosworldwide.com/)

[Explore EOS Academy](http://academy.eosworldwide.com/get-started)

## Here's What Happens Next

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-One.svg)

#### 15-Minute Fit Call

A quick call to understand your business and match you with the right EOS Implementer. No pressure. No pitch.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-Two.svg)

#### 90-Minute Meeting

A free, in-depth session with your matched Implementer. You'll walk through the EOS Model, identify your biggest issues, and decide if EOS is right for your team.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-Three.svg)

#### You Decide

If it's a fit, you start your EOS journey with a Focus Day. If not, you walk away with clarity on what your business needs. Either way, you win.

"He was relaxed and successful. I wasn't. EOS changed that."

##### Scott Seefeld

Titus Talent Strategies

"We argued about the same issue 19 times. EOS gave us a way to actually solve it."

##### Anthony Karls

Rock Clicks

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/icn-logo.svg)

## Frequently   Asked Questions

What does the 90-Minute Meeting cost?

Nothing. It's free.

Who will I meet with?

[A Professional, Certified or Expert EOS Implementer who has been certified in the full EOS system and matched to your industry, size, and geography. You can also browse the directory and choose your own.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-an-eos-implementer)

What do I need to prepare?

Nothing. Just show up ready to be honest about your business.

What if EOS isn't right for us?

Then we'll tell you. EOS isn't for every company, and we're honest about that. You leave with clarity either way.

## Book Your 90-Minute Meeting

All 90-Minute Meetings start with a brief 15-minute fit call. Schedule yours below.

data.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/tools#content "Skip to content")

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/eos-tool-2-scaled.jpg)

# THE EOS TOOLBOX

A complete set of simple, practical tools that strengthen the Six Key Components of your business. Every foundational tool is free to download.

[START HERE](https://www.eosworldwide.com/tools#five-foundational-tools)

## The Five Foundational Tools

These five tools create the foundation for running your business on EOS.

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-1-VTO.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto-download)

### [Vision/Traction Organizer](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto)

##### Start with clarity.

Answer the Eight Questions that define your company’s vision

- [Core Values](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-values)
- [Core Focus](https://www.eosworldwide.com/core-focus)
- [10-Year Target](https://www.eosworldwide.com/10-year-target)
- [Marketing Strategy](https://www.eosworldwide.com/marketing-strategy)
- [3-Year Picture](https://www.eosworldwide.com/3-year-picture)
- [1-Year Plan](https://www.eosworldwide.com/1-year-plan)
- [Quarterly Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks)
- [Issues List](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-list)

Your leadership team doesn’t move forward until you are 100% on the same page about where you're going and how you'll get there.

###### [Download the Free Tool](https://www.eosworldwide.com/vto-download)

#### Want a facilitator to guide your leadership team?

[Schedule a 90-Minute Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/get-started)

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx1-1.png)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-2-accountability.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart-download)

### [Accountability Chart](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart)

##### Clarify who is accountable for what.

This is not an org chart. It defines the right structure for accountability.

Build it using these principles:

- Structure first, people second
- Right People, Right Seats
- Maximum five roles per seat
- Visionary never has LMA

Without this clarity, execution becomes foggy.

###### [Download the Free Tool](https://www.eosworldwide.com/accountability-chart-download)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-3-scorecard.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard-download)

### [Scorecard](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard)

##### Create visibility with data.

Track 5–15 weekly leading indicators so problems appear early.

- Who owns it
- What is measured
- The weekly goal
- The weekly result

If you can’t see it weekly, you can’t manage it.

###### [Download the Free Tool](https://www.eosworldwide.com/scorecard-download)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-4-rocks.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks-download)

### [Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks)

##### Focus on what matters most.

Every 90 days, leaders set the 3–7 priorities that must get done.

Each Rock includes:

- Clear owner
- Clear result
- Clear deadline

This is where vision turns into execution.

###### [Download the Free Tool](https://www.eosworldwide.com/rocks-download)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-5-L10.jpg)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-level-10-meeting-download)

### [Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/level-10-meeting)

##### Install a disciplined weekly meeting.

Same day. Same time. Same agenda.

Your leadership team meets weekly to:

- Review the Scorecard
- Review Rocks
- Identify Issues
- IDS the most important ones

This is where issues get solved at the root.

###### [Download the Free Tool](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-level-10-meeting-download)

#### [The Organizational Checkup](https://organizationalcheckup.com/)

See how strong your business really is.

A 20-question diagnostic that scores your strength in each of the Six Key Components. Free. Takes 10 minutes. Get your score and a roadmap.

[Take the Checkup](https://organizationalcheckup.com/)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/gfx-checkup.png)](https://organizationalcheckup.com/)

## More Tools in the EOS Toolbox

These additional tools help leadership teams strengthen specific areas of the business.

##### [The EOS Toolbox](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-toolbox)

[The complete set of business management tools curated to strengthen any business.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-toolbox)

##### [The People Analyzer](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-people-analyzer-download)

[Evaluate whether the right people are in the right seats by measuring alignment with your Core Values and GWC™.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-people-analyzer-download)

##### [Delegate and Elevate](https://www.eosworldwide.com/delegate-and-elevate-tool-download)

[Focus leaders on the work they do best.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/delegate-and-elevate-tool-download)

##### [The Issues Solving Track](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-solving-track-download)

[Identify, discuss, and solve issues at the root.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/issues-solving-track-download)

##### [The 8 Cash Flow Drivers](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-8-cash-flow-drivers)

[Identify the key drivers of cash flow.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/the-8-cash-flow-drivers)

##### [Culture Checkup](https://www.eosworldwide.com/culture-checkup-dl)

[Assess the health and intentionality of your workplace culture and discover where you land on The Culture Quadrant.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/culture-checkup-dl)

##### [The EOS Model](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-model-download)

[Skip to content](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-history-team#content "Skip to content")

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Current-Leadership-Team-crop.png)

# About EOS® Worldwide

#### EOS Worldwide is a growing organization of successful entrepreneurs from a variety of backgrounds who are passionate about helping other business leaders succeed. The power of EOS is that it is real world, simple and practical. There’s no theory or flavor of the month, no magic pills—just timeless, field-tested tools that work.

#### With over 16 years of history and more than 275,000\+ companies currently using our tools, EOS is a proven system that consistently helps companies achieve more revenue, growth and profit while delivering a better balance of life to business owners and leaders.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/ebooks-icon.png)

EOS Books Sold

0+

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/thought-icon.png)

0+

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/toolbox-icon.png)

# The EOS Worldwide Leadership Team

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-MOD-Headshot-2.png)

### Mark O'Donnell

##### Visionary

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-Kelly-Headshot-1024x1024.png)

### Kelly Knight

##### Integrator

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-Amber-Headshot.png)

### Amber Baird

##### Content & Learning

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-Ben-Headshot_Glasses-Fix-1024x1024.png)

### Benjamin Ertischek

##### Finance & Administration

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/lauren_orange_backdrop_final.jpg)

### Lauren Shaver

##### Franchise Sales & Sponsorships

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-Jason-Headshot.png)

### Jason Jones

##### Marketing

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LT-Brian-Headshot-1024x1024.png)

### Brian Costanzo

##### Head of Community & Operations

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Lisa-Headshot.png)

### Lisa Hofmann

##### HR/Co. Administration

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Jennifer-EOS-819x1024.jpeg)

### Jennifer Yruegas

##### General Counsel

# Our Purpose and Core Values

EOS Worldwide exists to help people get what they want from their business.  We do that by developing master [EOS Implementers](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-an-eos-implementer) who are the best in the world at helping entrepreneurial leadership teams gain traction towards achieving everything in their vision.

We are abundance-minded and collaborative, working together to grow individually and help entrepreneurs worldwide.

#### **At our core, we are people who:**

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/humbly-confident-icon.png)

### Are Humbly Confident

open, honest, real and well-practiced, ready to make a positive difference

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/do-the-right-thing-icon-2.png)

### Do the Right Thing

never betraying a trust and doing whatever it takes to resolve every issue so people can move forward

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/grow-or-die-icon.png)

### Grow or Die

driven to maximize every situation and take ourselves and our clients to the next level

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/help-first-icon.png)

### Help First

subordinating our personal interests to advance others, always giving value before expecting anything in return

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/do-what-we-say-icon.png)

### Do What We Say

sometimes more, never less

# Our History

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/gino-square-150x150.jpg)

Our company founder, **[Gino Wickman](https://www.amazon.com/Gino-Wickman/e/B001JS19S4)**, started his entrepreneurial journey at age 21. After taking over the family business at age 25, he turned the company around and continued running it for another seven years before selling it. During that time, Gino became involved with the Detroit chapter of the Entrepreneurs’ Organization and found himself advising many of his fellow EO members. This led to the discovery that he had a passion for helping entrepreneurs and began the vision for what would become the Entrepreneurial Operating System®.

**Don Tinney**, a business veteran with over 30 years of experience leading entrepreneurial teams and our company’s co-founder, met Gino during the early stages of EOS’s development. After joining forces with Gino and reproducing his results in implementing EOS® with his own clients, Don became the second EOS Implementer®. Together, the two of them started to build the EOS Implementer Community™ before officially launching EOS Worldwide in 2008.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/06/Don_Tinney-150x150.jpg)

Today, Gino and Don are no longer involved in running EOS Worldwide but continue to champion entrepreneurship and engage with the community.

# A brief history of EOS

## 2000

Gino Wickman starts to create his system in September 2000.

He works with 50+ clients to refine the tools and system – calling it the Business Accelerator Model & Process.

## 2004

Gino is at a crossroads:

"Keep it to myself or leverage it?"

## 2005

Gino is introduced to Don Tinney. Don is completely different from Gino and Gino has doubts that Don can replicate his success.

Don sets out to prove him wrong.

## 2006

After reproducing every one of Gino's results, Don becomes the second EOS Implementer.

In June 2006, Gino renames his system to the **Entrepreneurial Operating System**.

## 2007

Gino and Don start recruiting EOS Implementers and hold the first Boot Camp in August 2007.

Gino self-publishes Tractionin September 2007.

## 2008

EOS Worldwide officially launches with eight EOS Implementers.

## 2009

The company is floundering. Gino and Don decide that if things don't get better by June, they will pull the plug.

They decide to implement a new, abundance-minded business model for the EOS Implementer Community.

## 2012

Gino and Mike Paton publish Get A Grip.

## 2015

Paton replaces Gino as Visionary of EOS Worldwide.

Gino and Mark Winters publish Rocket Fuel.

The Professional EOS Implementer Community grows to more than 100 members.

**_Traction_** is published in Chinese (Simplified).

## 2016

Kelly Knight replaces Don Tinney as Integrator and becomes EOS Worldwide's first full-time employee.

Rene Boer and Gino publish How to Be a Great Boss.

## 2017

First international Boot Camp is held in Sydney, Australia in March 2017.

First EOS Conference is held in April 2017.

Gino and Tom Bouwer publish _**What the Heck is EOS?**_

## 2018

**Firefly Group** become owners/partners in May 2018.

## 2019

**_Traction_** is published in Romanian and Vietnamese.

## 2020

Mark O'Donnell replaces Paton as Visionary.

Virtual sessions, QCEs, and Boot Camps become a new way to reach new organizations, stay safe, and increase flexibility.

**_Traction_** is published in Korean.

## 2021

EOS transitions from a membership model to a Franchise model and completes a full company rebrand.

Gino Wickman publishes **_The EOS Life_**, making it the sixth book in the Traction Library.

**_Traction_** is published in Portuguese (Brazil).

## 2022

EOS One Beta launched in January 2022.

Mike Paton and Lisa González published Process!.

Strong in Six London is the first client event held outside of North America.

**_Traction_** is published in Mongolian, French, and Japanese.

## 2023

New & Improved EOS One work begins.

First-ever “We Run on EOS” events introduced, Strong in Six Sydney and Self-Implementer Workshop launch.

Record “people numbers.” 100+ EOSW staff, 750+ total EOS Implementers (177 joined in 2023), 85+ Rocket Fuel IMF members.

**_Traction_** is published in Dutch and Russian.

## 2024

EOS One launches in April 2024.

Mark O'Donnell, Kelly Knight, and CJ Dube' publish _**People**_.

**_Traction_** is published in Africa/Haiti French and Arabic.
