#### Other Helpful Links

- [Master Terms of Use](https://www.eosworldwide.com/terms-conditions/)
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy/)
- [California Applicant Privacy Notice](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy/california)
- [Your Privacy Choices](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy#)

#### Last Updated: October 28, 2024

## EOS Worldwide Privacy Notice

We may provide you with access to certain EOS Worldwide systems, programs, products, and other intellectual property, which include (i) websites (including https://www.eosworldwide.com and https://rocketfueluniversity.com), software (including EOS One™), platforms, subscriptions, memberships, applications (including mobile applications), tools, and content that we may provide or make available from time-to-time; (ii) email and network systems and accounts; (iii) client management platforms and wireless high-speed Internet access systems; and  (iv) e-commerce (such as our EOS Online Store) and promotions (collectively, the “EOS Web Properties”).

This EOS Worldwide Privacy Notice (this “Privacy Notice”) describes the personal information that EOS Worldwide, LLC, a Michigan limited liability company and its applicable EOS affiliated entities (“EOS Worldwide”, “we”, “us”, “our”) collect when you interact with us through all of our EOS Web Properties that display or link to this notice or through offline services, how we use it, with whom we share it, how long we retain it, and your choices in connection with it.

This Privacy Notice does not apply to information collected by any third party, including through any application or content (including advertising) that may link to or be accessible from or through the EOS Web Properties, unless such information is shared with us for the purposes we describe below.  Once you leave our EOS Web Properties, you will be subject to third party terms and conditions and privacy policies.

You should read this Privacy Notice and the [EOS Worldwide General Terms of Use](https://www.eosworldwide.com/terms-conditions/general) (“General Terms”) carefully before accessing or using any EOS Web Property or sharing any personal information with us. Capitalized terms used herein and not otherwise defined shall have the meaning set forth in the General Terms.

This Privacy Notice may change from time to time. and we will notify you of such changes by displaying or linking an updated version of this Privacy Notice on our EOS Web Properties and procuring your consent, when needed.

**COLLECTION OF YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION**

We collect personal information, which is any information that identifies, relates to, describes, is capable of being associated with, or could reasonably be linked, directly or indirectly, to you, when you engage with EOS Worldwide or our EOS Web Properties. Specifically, we collect personal information both directly from you and automatically as you navigate and use our EOS Web Properties, each as described below.

Specifically, depending on your relationship with us, we collect and process the following categories of Personal Information:

- Information by which you may be personally identified, such as name, postal address, email address, telephone number, visual information (e.g., photograph, if provided), or other identifiers by which you may be contacted online or offline (“ _personal identifiers_”);
- Information that is demographic in nature, such as date of birth, gender and marital status (“demographic _information_”);
- Information should you provide it that is considered sensitive information, such as Social Security or other state identification number (only under limited circumstances); geolocation; racial or ethnic origin, religious or philosophical beliefs (“ _sensitive personal identifiers_“);
- Information that is about your professional history or experiences, such as your resume, cover letter, educational history, employment history, professional affiliations, role or title, company information and specialized certifications (“ _professional or employment-related information_”);
- Information should you provide it, that we may use to process payments, such as credit card and debit card information (“ _payment information_”);
- Information that is used to access your account on any of our EOS Web Properties (“ _account information_”); and
- Information about your internet or other electronic network activity, such as IP address, location information based on the IP address, Internet connection, date and time of the server request, device and browser data, time zone, specific browser or app function, access status, amount of data transferred, log data, operating system used, and its interface (e.g., Android or IOS), language, version of the operating system, device identifiers, and other usage details (“ _internet or other electronic network activity information_”).

For Personal Information that is not automatically collected, you may decide not to provide this information to us. However, if you do not provide it to us , it may affect our ability to provide you with products, services, information or assistance you have requested. For Personal Information which is automatically collected, you may also exercise certain privacy rights as described in this Policy, but that exercise may also effect our ability to provide you with services or functionality on the EOS Web Properties.

**(A) Information Collected Directly From You**

For all visitors, the Personal Information we collect through our EOS Web Properties will include, as appropriate:

- information that you provide by filling in forms on our websites and other EOS Web Properties. This includes information provided at the time of registering to use our EOS Web Properties, subscribing to our services, posting material, or requesting further services. We may also ask you for information when you enter a promotion sponsored by us, and when you report a problem or request support from us.
- records and copies of your correspondence (including email addresses), if and when you contact us.
- your responses to surveys or other assessments that we might ask you to complete for research type purposes.
- details of transactions you carry out through our EOS Web Properties and of the fulfillment of your orders. You may be required to provide _payment information_ before placing an order through our EOS Web Properties or when signing up for event or a class.
- your search queries on our EOS Web Properties.

You also have the ability to provide information to be published or displayed (hereinafter, “posted”) on public areas or semi-public areas accessible to certain members of our EOS Worldwide communities, or transmitted to other users of our EOS Web Properties or third parties (collectively, “User Contributions”). Your User Contributions are posted on and transmitted to others at your own risk. Additionally, we cannot control the actions of other users with whom you may choose to share your User Contributions.

Outside of the above general collection activities, we collect personal information directly from you when you:

- **Apply for employment.** If you apply for employment with EOS Worldwide, we will collect your _personal identifiers_, _demographic information_, certain _sensitive personal identifiers_, and your _professional or employment-related information_. For more information on how we handle California applicants’ personal information, please see our [EOS Worldwide California Applicant Privacy Notice](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy/california).
- **Attend one of our events.** If you register to attend one our EOSW Events ( _such as an EOS Conference™, a QCE™ (Quarterly Collaborative Exchange™), a QIE™ (Quarterly Integrator Exchange™), a Rocket Fuel™ Masterclass, or a Great Boss™ Workshop)_, we collect your _personal identifiers_, certain _demographic information_, certain _sensitive personal identifiers_, and certain _professional or employment-related information_, and if you provide it, your _payment information_. We may collect this information directly or you may be directed to our third-party providers that help us manage registrations.
- **Become an EOS Implementer®.** If you wish to become an EOS Implementer, you will need to complete an application. We collect from you your _personal iden_ _t_ _ifiers_, certain _demographic information_, _sensitive personal identifiers_, your _professional or employment-related information_ and have you answer a series of questions related to your desire to become an EOS Implementer.
- **Find an EOS Implementer.** If you wish to use the find an EOS Implementer feature on our EOS Web Properties, you will fill out an online form. We will collect from you your _personal identifiers_ and your _professional or employment-related information_. We may record these calls, however if we do you will be informed before the recording begins.
- **Create a company account.** If you create a company or business account with us (including through our EOS One™ Software, Base Camp, or within Rocket Fuel University®), we will collect your _personal identifiers_ and your _professional or employment-related information_.
- **Create an individual account**. If you create an individual account with us (including through our EOS One™ Software or within Rocket Fuel University®), we will collect your _personal identifiers_ and certain _professional or employment-related information_. We may supplement the information that we receive from you with other aggregated information that we get from third-party sources to enrich your profile so that we can tailor our marketing communications in accordance with your interests.
- **Participate in the Rocket Fuel™ V/I Connector Program.** If you participate in the Rocket Fuel™ V/I Connector Program, we will collect your _personal identifiers_ and certain _professional or employment-related information_. We may supplement the information that we receive from you with information that we get from third-party assessment tools such as The Kolbe A Index and The Working Genius Assessment to enrich your profile so that we can compile your data and assessment results, add you to our pool, and share your information with participating Visionaries/companies when we identify you as a potential top match.
- **Contact us.** When you send us a question or inquiry, use the chat function if available on any of our EOS Web Properties, or ask for other support, you will need to provide us with _personal identifiers_ and possibly _your_ _account information_ under certain circumstances.
- **Host an event**. EOS Implementers are permitted to host certain EOS events from time to time. If you host an EOS event, we may collect your _personal identifiers_ and if applicable, _payment information_.
- **Interact with our Community Message Boards**. If you interact and post on our community message boards (e.g., EOS Pulse), we will collect your _personal identifiers_ as well as any information that you provide when interacting with these message boards by commenting, sharing and rating.
- **Order From the EOS Online Store**. If you choose to purchase any products or materials from our EOS Online Store, we may collect your _personal identifiers_. We may collect this information directly or you may be directed to our third-party providers that help us manage our online sales. We collect all the purchase information, besides the _payment information_, from our third-party event providers.
- **Subscribe to our EOS Blogs.** If you subscribe to our blogs for updates, news, and special offers, we will collect your _personal identifiers_. Our emails include a tracking pixel that tracks whether you open the email and whether you click banners in the email and your subsequent clicks and usage of our website.
- **Interact with us on social media**. When you interact with our fan page on social networking websites, such as Meta (including its platforms, such known as Facebook and Instagram), X (formally known as Twitter), and LinkedIn (each a “Page” and collectively “Pages”), we may collect basic engagement metrics and use it to tailor content and marketing and to improve user experience as set forth in this section. Please understand that we do not control the use or storage of the information that you have posted to any third-party social networking websites. This information is collected and processed by the social networking websites for their own purposes, including marketing.
- **Pages**. When interacting with our Pages, we may collect from you, your _personal identifiers_ as well as any information that you provide when interacting with our Pages (e.g., commenting, sharing and rating). We use this information to advertise our products, for events and invitations, and to communicate with users via the contribution and comment function.

Because our Pages are publicly accessible, when you use them to interact with other users, for example by posting, leaving comments or liking or sharing posts, any personal information that you post in them or provide when registering can be viewed by others or used by them as they see fit.

- **Community Management.** With the help of a third party, we may collect, from you, your contact, including “likes”, shares, messages and other interactions with the content.
- **Surveys**. When you participate in a survey on our Facebook Page, we collect, from you, your _personal_ _identifiers_, your _internet or other electronic network activity information_, and metadata (usage patterns).

We also use the personal information we collect as described in this section to comply with the law, to efficiently maintain our business, as described below in **HOW WE USE YOUR INFORMATION**, and for the other limited circumstances as described below in **HOW WE SHARE YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION**.

**(B)** **Information Collected Automatically**

As you navigate through and interact with our EOS Web Properties, we may use automatic data collection technologies to collect certain information about your equipment, browsing actions, and patterns, including:

- details of your visits to our EOS Web Properties, including traffic data, location data, logs, and other communication data and the resources that you access and use with our EOS Web Properties.
- Information about your computer and internet connection, including your IP address, operating system, and browser type.

The technologies we use for this automatic data collection may include:

- **Cookies (or browser cookies)**. A cookie is a small file placed on the hard drive of your computer. You may refuse to accept browser cookies by activating the appropriate setting on your browser. However, if you select this setting you may be unable to access certain parts of our EOS Web Properties. Unless you have adjusted your browser setting so that it will refuse cookies, our system will issue cookies when you direct your browser to our EOS Web Properties.
- **Web Beacons**. Pages of our EOS Web Properties may contain small electronic files known as web beacons (also referred to as clear gifs, pixel tags, and single-pixel gifs) that permit EOS Worldwide, for example, to count users who have visited those pages or opened an email and for other related website statistics (for example, recording the popularity of certain website content and verifying system and server integrity).

**Our use of Cookies and other Tracking Technologies when you Interact with the EOS Web Properties.**

We use necessary (essential), functional, measurement (analytics), and marketing (advertising and targeting), cookies to collect your usage, device, and location information when you interact with the EOS Web Properties.

You can manage your cookie preference by visiting [Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy#) One particular third-party cookie to note that we use on our EOS Web Properties: **Google Analytics** (please see below for more information regarding how we use **Google Analytics**). In general, you can also disable cookies by setting your browser to refuse cookies or indicate when a cookie is being sent. Please note, if you opt out of these targeted advertising cookies, your opt-out will be specific to the web browser, app, or device from which you accessed the opt-out. If you use multiple devices or web browsers, you will need to opt out of each browser or device that you use.

As noted above, we use **Google Analytics** to collect information on your use of our EOS Web Properties to improve our websites and mobile applications. In order to collect this information, **Google Analytics** may set cookies on your browser or read cookies that are already there. **Google Analytics** may also receive information about you from applications you have downloaded that partner with Google. We do not combine the information collected through the use of **Google Analytics** with personal information. Google’s ability to use and share information collected by **Google Analytics** about your visits to our EOS Web Properties or to other applications which partner with Google is restricted by the **Google Analytics** Terms of Use and Privacy Notice. To prevent your data from being used by **Google Analytics**, you can download the Google Analytics opt-out browser add-on and/or follow the instructions in the **YOUR INFORMATION CHOICES** section below.

**HOW WE USE YOUR INFORMATION**

We use information that we collect about you or that you provide to us, including any personal information:

- To present our EOS Web Properties and their contents to you.
- To provide you with information, products, or services that you request from us.
- To fulfill the purpose described when you provide your Personal Information.
- To provide you with notices about your account or subscription, including expiration and renewal notices.
- To evaluate your eligibility for employment with us.
- To carry out our obligations and enforce our rights arising from any contracts entered into between you and us, including for billing and collection.
- To protect EOS Worldwide, our site visitors, our implementers, our customers, and other third party’s rights.
- To notify you about changes to our EOS Web Properties or any products or services we offer or provide through it.
- To allow you to participate in interactive features on our EOS Web Properties.
- To fulfill our legal obligations.
- To evaluate and execute on business transactions such as a merger, acquisition, or similar circumstance
- where all or some of our assets will be sold.
- In any other way we may describe in this Privacy Notice or when you provide the information.

The information we collect automatically may include personal information, but we may maintain it or associate it with personal information we also collect in other ways or receive from third parties. It helps us to improve our EOS Web Properties and to deliver a better and more personalized service, including by enabling us to:

- Track you within the EOS Web Properties.
- Estimate our audience size and usage patterns.
- Store information about your preferences, allowing us to customize our EOS Web Properties according to your individual interests and enhance your user experience.
- Speed up your searches.
- Conduct analytics to improve the EOS Web Properties.
- Recognize you when you return to our EOS Web Properties.
- Prevent fraudulent use of the EOS Web Properties.
- Diagnosis and repair errors, and, in cases of abuse, track and mitigate the abuse.
- Market to you more effectively across different web pages and social media platforms based on your browsing habits and history.

For the Rocket Fuel™ V/I Connector Program, we will engage in a process commonly known as profiling, in which we will process your personal data and the results of your assessments to help us evaluate, analyze, and predict whether you are a good fit for a Visionary/company. We do this through the use of certain algorithms and formulas we developed as well as leveraging the expertise and experience of our Rocket Fuel team. This process takes into account personal preferences, interests, and other personal data that you provide. If you are selected as a possible match, your personal information will be sent to the corresponding Visionary/company. This information will include your _personal identifiers_ and _professional or employment-related information_, and your assessment results. If you do not wish to have your personal information profiled or shared as part of this program, then please do not participate.

We may also use your information to contact you about our own and third parties’ goods and services that may be of interest to you. For more information, see **YOUR INFORMATION CHOICES** below.

We may use the information we have collected from you to enable us to display advertisements to our advertisers’ target audiences. Even though we do not disclose your personal information for these purposes without your consent, if you click on or otherwise interact with an advertisement, the advertiser may assume that you meet its target criteria.

**HOW WE SHARE YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION**

**General Sharing**

We may need to make personal information identified in this Privacy Notice available within EOS Worldwide, with service providers, EOS Implementers, or with other third parties as set forth below.  We may also disclose aggregated information about our users, and information that does not identify any individual, without restriction.

**Within EOS Worldwide.** We may share your personal information with our affiliates, employees, agents, and representatives for legitimate business purposes, general business management, and as permitted by law. We may also share your personal information with our affiliates, employees, agents, and representatives for marketing purposes. If you are a California resident and wish to opt out, please follow the instructions in the **YOUR INFORMATION CHOICES** section.

**With EOS Implementers.**  We may share your personal information with our EOS Implementers and their affiliates, employees, agents and representatives for our legitimate business purposes as permitted by law, including, if you use the “Find an Implementer” feature on our EOS Web Properties. We may also share your personal information with our EOS Implementers and their affiliates, employees, agents, and representatives for marketing purposes.

**With Contractors or Service Providers.**  We may share your personal information with our or our EOS Implementers’ service providers that assist us in providing EOS products or services or otherwise support our businesses. These service providers include payment processors, cloud service providers, online form providers, ticketing providers, communications vendors, website providers, marketing providers, shipping providers, payment processors, and online scheduling providers.

**With other** **jurisdictions.** We may disclose personal information outside of the jurisdiction from which it was collected. In the conduct of our business, we transfer to, hold or access personal information from various countries including the United States, Canada, Australia, and the EU.

**YOUR INFORMATION CHOICES**

We strive to provide you with choices regarding the personal information you provide to us. We have created mechanisms to provide you with the following control over your information:

- **Correct or View Your Information.** You may access your account within our applicable EOS Web Properties to correct or view certain personal information you have provided to us and which is associated with your account.
- **Opt Out of Advertising Cookies.** To set your cookie preferences or opt out of the sale or sharing of your information via cookies, please visit [Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy#). All session cookies are temporary and expire after you close your web browser. Persistent cookies can be removed by following your web browser’s directions. To find out how to see what cookies have been set on your computer or device, and how to reject and delete the cookies, please visit: [https://www.aboutcookies.org/](https://www.aboutcookies.org/). Please note that each web browser is different. To find information relating to your browser, visit the browser developer’s website and mobile application. If you reset your web browser to refuse all cookies or to indicate when a cookie is being sent, some features of our website may not function properly. If you choose to opt out, we will place an “opt-out cookie” on your device. The “opt-out cookie” is browser specific and device specific and only lasts until cookies are cleared from your browser or device. The opt-out cookie will not work for essential cookies. If the cookie is removed or deleted, if you upgrade your browser or if you visit us from a different computer, you will need to return and update your preferences. By clicking on the “Opt-Out” links below, you will be directed to the respective third-party website where your computer will be scanned to determine who maintains cookies on you. At that time, you can either choose to opt out of all targeted advertising or you can choose to opt out of targeted advertising by selecting individual companies who maintain a cookie on your machine.

For more information about reject and delete advertising cookies, please see:

- Association of National Advertisers opt-out registration: [https://dmachoice.thedma.org/](https://dmachoice.thedma.org/)
- Network Advertising Initiative (NAI) Opt-Out: [https://www.networkadvertising.org/managing/opt\_out.asp](https://www.networkadvertising.org/managing/opt_out.asp)
- Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA) Opt-Out: [https://optout.aboutads.info](https://optout.aboutads.info/)
- European Union (EU) /European Economic Area (EEA) Opt-Out: [http://www.youronlinechoices.eu](http://www.youronlinechoices.eu/)
- In general, you can also disable cookies by setting your browser to refuse cookies or indicate when a cookie is being sent.
- **Opt Out of Google Analytics.** To prevent your data from being used by **Google Analytics**, you can download the **Google Analytics** opt-out browser.
- **Opt Out of Other Cookies.** You can manage your cookie preferences, including opting out, at any time, by clicking [![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/privacy-icon.svg) YOUR PRIVACY CHOICES](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy#)

- **Opt Out of Email Tracking.** You can disable this tracking by blocking automatic loading of images in your email. You may also click the “unsubscribe” link provided in our emails at any time.
- **Opt Out of Marketing Communications.** You may opt out of receiving marketing emails from us by clicking the “unsubscribe” link provided with each email. Please note that we will continue to send you notifications necessary to your account, purchases, our services or the EOS Web Properties, or any assistance you request.

We do not control third parties’ collection or use of your information to serve interest-based advertising. However these third parties may provide you with ways to choose not to have your information collected or used in this way. You can opt out of receiving targeted ads from members of the Network Advertising Initiative ( **NAI**) on the NAI’s website.

**DO NOT TRACK**

As Do Not Track (DNT) and Global Privacy Controls (GPC) are not widely recognized, we do not respond to DNT or GPC signals. These signals are triggered by a preference you can set in your web browser to inform Website and mobile applications that you do not want to be tracked.

**Text Messaging and Auto-Dial Communications.** We may contact you by telephone or text message (including through the use of artificial voices, prerecorded voice messages and/or autodialed calls and text messages) to the telephone number you provide or to any number provided to us on your behalf, for various purposes including verifying your identity, providing you with important notices regarding your account or use of our services, fulfilling your requests or letting you know about promotions or other services we think we may be of interest to you. Your consent to receive automated calls and texts is completely voluntary, and you may opt out any time. You acknowledge that if you do not opt out, we may contact you even if your number is listed on a do-not-call list or if you cancel your account or terminate your relationship with us. You do not have to agree to receive promotional calls or texts as a condition of purchasing any goods or services.

For any text messages sent to you in connection with the EOS Web Properties: (a) message frequency may vary, (b) message and data rates may apply, and we are not responsible for these charges, (c) you may reply HELP when available or contact us for information, (d) you can reply STOP when available or contact us to opt out at any time (though if you do, you agree to receive a single message confirming your opt-out) and (e) neither EOS Worldwide nor mobile carriers involved in the text messaging are liable for delayed or undelivered messages.

To opt out of text messaging or automated voice calls (other than as permitted through the service), you must provide us with written notice revoking your consent by contacting us as described below or in Section 12(f) of our General Terms of Use and including your full name and the specific phone number(s) you wish to opt out of such calls. You may continue to receive service-related text messages from other phone numbers managed by EOS Worldwide, and you may opt out of those in a similar fashion.

Without your consent, with the exception of originator opt-in data and consent information that may be shared in accordance with this Privacy Notice, no mobile information will be shared with or sold to third parties or affiliates or otherwise used for marketing or promotional purposes.

**YOUR PRIVACY RIGHTS**

Various state and international privacy laws may provide their residents with additional rights regarding our use of their personal information. To learn more about consumer privacy rights in these states and how to exercise them, please see below.

To the extent a general privacy law applies to our processing of your personal information, you are generally entitled to the following rights:

- **Right to Access/Know.** You may have the right to request what personal information we have collected, used, disclosed, and sold about you. Note that this right is not absolute. We may deny your right of access where such access might compromise the privacy rights of another person, or where providing access proves impossible or would involve disproportionate effort. You may only make a request for access twice within a 12-month period. To exercise your right to access your personal information, you may submit a request by following the instructions below.
- **Right to Deletion.** You may have the right to request the deletion of your personal information that we collect or maintain, subject to certain exceptions. For example, if we are required by law to retain the information that you are asking to be deleted, we would not be able to delete the information until we are legally permitted to delete it. To exercise your right to delete your personal information, you may submit a request by following the instructions below.
- **Right to Opt Out of Sale/Sharing.** You may have the right to opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information to third parties for marketing purposes. We do not have actual knowledge that we sell or share personal information of minors under the age of sixteen (16) years. To exercise your right to opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information, you may submit a request by following the instructions below.
- **Right to Non-Discrimination.** You may have the right to not receive discriminatory treatment if and when you exercise your rights to access, delete, opt out, correct, or limit use of sensitive personal information (as defined below).
- **Right to Correct:** You may have the right to correct inaccurate personal information that we collect or maintain.You can exercise this right by logging into your EOS Worldwide account and updating the information on your account, or by following the instructions below.
- **Right to data portability.** You may have the right to ask that we transfer the personal data you gave us from one organization to another or give it to you. However, you make exercise this right no more than two times per calendar year.
- **Right to Limit Use of Sensitive Personal Information** **_:_** _Sensitive personal information_ is personal information that reveals (a) consumer’s Social Security or other state identification number; (b) a consumer’s account log-in, financial account, debit card, or credit card number in combination with any required security or access code, password, or credentials allowing access to an account; (c) consumer’s geolocation; (d) consumer’s racial or ethnic origin, religious or philosophical beliefs, or union membership; (e) the contents of a consumer’s mail, email, or text messages, unless the business is the intended recipient of the communication; and (f) consumer’s genetic data. Examples include, but are not limited to, driver’s license number, biometric information, and precise geolocation.You may have the right to limit the use of your _sensitive personal information_ when such use goes beyond that which is necessary for providing our products and services to you, the EOS Web Properties, or for certain other permissible purposes like fraud, customer service or quality control. We do not use _sensitive personal information_ collected through the EOS Web Properties in this manner.
- **Right to appeal.** You may have the right to appeal an action taken (or not taken) by EOS Worldwide in response to your request. To exercise your right to appeal, please email and provide, in detail, the contents of your original request, the reason the request was denied, a detailed explanation on why the request should be granted, and any other information you wish for us to consider. Please use the subject line “Consumer Right Appeal Request” and please indicate which state you are exercising this appeal under. For example, “Virginia Consumer Right Appeal Request.” If we fail to reverse our decision, we will provide you with the proper contact information to file a complaint with the appropriate governmental authority.

To exercise your right to access, correct, or delete your personal information, or to opt out of the sale of your personal information for marketing purposes, you may submit a request by completing this [form](https://formstack.io/8A796) in its entirety. To opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information via cookies, please visit [Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information.](https://www.eosworldwide.com/privacy-policy#)

For requests submitted, you must provide us with sufficient information that allows us to reasonably verify your identity, as well as the right you are exercising. This information includes your name, email address, physical address, and please indicate the nature of your relationship with EOS Worldwide (customer, EOS implementer, etc.).

The information you provide allows us to reasonably verify you are the person about whom we collected the personal information and describe your request with sufficient detail to allow us to properly evaluate and respond to it. In doing so, we will take steps to verify your request by matching information provided by you with the information we have in our records. If we are not able to verify your identity for access and deletion requests with the information provided, we may ask you for additional pieces of information.

Only you, or a person that you authorize to act on your behalf may make a request related to your personal information. If you are an authorized agent making a request on behalf of another individual, you must provide us with signed documentation that you are authorized to act on behalf of that individual. Please submit the signed authorization when prompted on your submission form.

You may also submit requests by emailing us at [privacy@eosworldwide.com](mailto:privacy@eosworldwide.com) or by contacting us toll-free at 877-367-1877. For email submissions, please write, “\[STATE\] Privacy Request,” in the subject of the email. For authorized agents, please include in the subject line: “\[STATE\] Privacy Request: Authorized Agent Information.”

**INFORMATION SECURITY**

We may hold the information we have collected in paper and/or electronic form.

We implement and maintain reasonable security measures, such as access controls and encryption, to protect the personal information that we collect and maintain from accidental loss, unauthorized access, destruction, use, modification, or disclosure.

Unfortunately, the transmission of information via the internet is not completely secure. Although we do our best to protect your personal information, we cannot guarantee the security of your personal information transmitted to our EOS Web Properties or any third-party sites. No security measure or modality of data transmission is 100% secure, and we are unable to guarantee the absolute security of the personal information we have collected from you.

Therefore, any transmission of personal information is at your own risk. We are not responsible for circumvention of any privacy settings or security measures contained on our EOS Web Properties or any third-party sites.

**DATA RETENTION**

We retain your personal information (i) for as long as the relevant EOS account exists, (ii) until we no longer need your information to fulfill the purposes for which we collected it, or (iii) until we receive a valid request to delete the information, in which case we will delete or anonymize the information within 30 days after receiving the request. However, we may need to use and retain your personal information for longer than the periods indicated above for purposes of:

- **Compliance with our legal obligations.** For example, retaining your records for the purpose of accounting, dispute resolution, and compliance with labor, tax, and financial regulations.
- **Meeting our safety and security commitments.** Such as keeping our properties secure and preventing fraud.
- **Exercising or defending legal claims.** We also may need to retain personal information for longer than the periods indicated above in order to respond to legal process or enforceable governmental requests, or to enforce our contracts or Terms of Use, including investigation of potential violations.

**CHANGES TO THIS PRIVACY NOTICE**

We may amend this Privacy Notice in our sole discretion at any time. If we do, we will post the changes to this page, and will indicate the date the changes go into effect. We encourage you to review our Privacy Notice to stay informed. If we make changes that materially affect Your Privacy Choices, we will notify you by prominent posting on our EOS Web Properties and/or via email, and obtain your consent, if required.

**CONTACT US**

If you have any questions or concerns on this Privacy Notice, please contact us by at [privacy@eosworldwide.com](mailto:support@eosworldwide.com) or by telephone at 877-367-1877.

## Pieter Winne

##### Certified EOS Implementer®

##### [Visit Website](https://www.eosworldwide.com/pieter-winne)

[Twitter](https://twitter.com/EOSWorldwide)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pieterdwinne/)

"My huge difference might be measured differently than somebody else's. My huge difference is we save one life."

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Screenshot-2024-07-02-132912.png)

Certified EOS Implementer® Pieter D. Winne founded HOOAH Inc., a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting deployed servicemen and women, their stateside families, and returning veterans. His “Ranger Shoot, Eat, and Drink” fundraising event started small eight years ago but has grown to raise $20,000 in 2023! The proceeds from this event are used to support veterans who suffer from post-traumatic stress, covering therapy costs and all of the “in-between” expenses it takes to get them there, such as travel costs, lost income due to time away from work, and more.

## Jill Young

##### Expert EOS Implementer™

##### [Visit Website](https://www.eosworldwide.com/jill-young)

[Twitter](https://x.com/tractionfirst?lang=en)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eoscoach/)

" If I can inspire other people to make a big difference, then that’s the big difference I want to make in the world. It’s so joyful and of course the EOS Life is a catalyst for that."

When I Can Still Shine, a foundation supporting women and children affected by domestic violence, lost one of its supporting organizations, Expert EOS Implementer™ and EOS Worldwide Head Coach Jill Young, rose to the occasion. For 15 years, she’s gathered hundreds of helpers to donate money and purchase Christmas gifts for the affected families. She also puts a portion of all her EOS session profits toward the cause. In 2023, her efforts amounted to $20,000 worth of toys!

## Catherine Juon

##### Certified EOS Implementer®

##### [Visit Website](https://www.eosworldwide.com/catherine-juon)

[Twitter](https://www.twitter.com/cjuon)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/cjuon/)

"I want to be able to make it possible that organizations who want to run on EOS should be able to run on EOS and I’m hopeful that that’s somewhere on our not too distant horizon."

Certified EOS Implementer Catherine Juon was introduced to Lee Willwerth, Visionary and CEO of DreamMaker Bath & Kitchen, to help nonprofit organizations, that are dedicated to supporting youth in underserved communities, learn how to operate better by Running on EOS™. With Catherine’s coaching and facilitation skills, nonprofit organizations are creating a better future for thousands of children through financial help, education, professional training, and more.

In addition, Catherine and Lee co-founded a new nonprofit focused on strategic business work to help visionaries around the world who aspire to be catalysts for transformational change: [Empowering Change](https://www.empoweringchange.org/).

##### EOS ONE™

## Jodi Owczarski

##### President & CEO

##### Michigan West Coast Chamber of Commerce

##### [Visit Website](https://www.westcoastchamber.org/)

[Twitter](https://twitter.com/EOSWorldwide)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/michigan-west-coast-chamber-of-commerce/)

"All of a sudden it was just that lightbulb moment where you realize, we could do something extraordinary here. "

Jodi Owczarski, President and CEO of the Michigan West Coast Chamber of Commerce, created Community Impact Day, a day for West Michigan residents to volunteer en masse at various community locations. Since its public introduction in 2022, the event has increased from 250 participants to 984 participants in 2023 and to a goal of 1,500 participants in 2024. From blood drives to natural area cleanup, a ton of work is done to help the community thrive — all in just 24 hours.

"We spend our time throwing pebbles in the water, and hoping that they have a lasting effect. I’ve come to appreciate the value of that ripple effect and the power of it. That things that you do today can have a lasting effect over potentially generations"

Lee Willwerth, Visionary and CEO of DreamMaker Bath & Kitchen, was a founding member of Care Village Outreach, a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting youth in underserved communities. With the help of Expert EOS Implementer™ Marisa Smith, Lee and his team’s growth allowed them to create a better future for thousands of children through financial help, education, professional training, and more.

Today, working with Certified EOS Implementer® Catherine Juon, Lee has co-founded a new nonprofit focused on strategic business work to help visionaries around the world who aspire to be catalysts for transformational change: [Empowering Change](https://www.empoweringchange.org/).

- [EOS Mastery](https://www.eosworldwide.com/category/blog)

# What is YOUR Moon Shot?

[![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/ad6e934e19006c2074efecf7e28d7ec16b73043bb78138bf73083710cc6cc168?s=96&d=mm&r=g)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/michael-halperin)

- [Michael Halperin](https://www.eosworldwide.com/michael-halperin)

![10-year target](https://www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/EOS_MasteryBlog_YOURMoonShot.jpg)

Vision in [EOS®](https://www.eosworldwide.com/) is about alignment – making sure everyone on the leadership team and, ultimately, everyone in the organization is crystal clear where the organization is going and how it will get there.

[During Vision Building™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/eos-process), we answer [eight questions](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/105107-eos-8-powerful-questions-crystallize-company-vision) that together define the company’s vision, providing that alignment. One of those eight is what I call the Finish Line to the Marathon: [the 10-Year Target™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/9711-traction-thoughts-setting-10-year-target-business).

In his book [_Good to Great_](https://www.amazon.com/Good-Great-Some-Companies-Others/dp/0066620996/ref=sr_1_1?crid=2RHUS6Z7W1BUA&dchild=1&keywords=good+to+great+by+jim+collins&qid=1620316112&sprefix=good+to+great%2Caps%2C195&sr=8-1), Jim Collins tells us that great companies are great goal setters. And it begins with what he calls a BHAG: a Big, Hairy, Audacious Goal. The 10-Year Target is a company’s BHAG. But it is also much more than that.

## Getting Everyone On Board With Your Vision

When you cascade your vision down through your organization, the 10-Year Target does two very important things:

1. It makes sure that everyone is marching in the same direction. In every organization, there are lots of important goals. But the 10-Year Target is one REALLY BIG goal… the one thing everyone in the organization needs to support and align with. In many organizations, people may have different and varying ideas about what the organization should do (and some of those ideas are driven by natural, ambitious self-interest). The 10-Year Target ensures that all of those ideas and ambitions are aligned to a single, common purpose. If someone’s pet project is going to accelerate the company’s ability to achieve this one, overarching goal, then tell them to put their ears back and run! But if it isn’t, then they need to step back, re-align, and get on board with where you are going.
2. It gives people the energy to march. The 10-Year Target should be aspirational AND inspirational. It should have an “AHA” effect that leads the right people for your organization to think “Yeah, I want to be part of a company that does that” (it also can help the “wrong people” realize they don’t belong… but that’s for another blog post).

## Every Organization Has Its Ups and Downs

It is often easy to keep people moving forward when things are going well. But during the lulls and the challenging times, the 10-Year Target can be a beacon that gives your people the energy to keep marching in the face of adversity. And it gives leadership confidence that your people are all marching in the same direction.

There has probably never been a more aspirational and inspirational 10-Year Target than [U.S. President John F. Kennedy’s “Moon Shot Challenge”](https://airandspace.si.edu/stories/editorial/we-choose-go-moon-jfks-moon-shot): to put a man on the moon and return him safely to earth. That target inspired a nation.

What 10-Year Target defines your “Moon Shot”?

_Previously published on the_ [_Freebridge blog_](https://www.freebridgeinc.com/what-is-your-moon-shot)

## Next Steps:

- [Download a copy of the EOS V/TO](https://traction.eosworldwide.com/download-vto) ™ to help you clarify, simplify, and achieve your vision
- Learn to make decisions faster and better by [downloading a copy of our free eBook, _Decide!_](https://www.eosworldwide.com/decide-book)
- Request a [free 90 Minute Meeting](https://traction.eosworldwide.com/90mm) with an EOS Implementer® to get a clear picture of what it looks like to run your company on EOS

[![Decide E-Book](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/434170/abbe3501-30cd-400b-9994-bf178fb2b0a3.png)](https://cta-redirect.hubspot.com/cta/redirect/434170/abbe3501-30cd-400b-9994-bf178fb2b0a3)

[![Picture of Michael Halperin](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/ad6e934e19006c2074efecf7e28d7ec16b73043bb78138bf73083710cc6cc168?s=300&d=mm&r=g)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/michael-halperin)

[**Michael Halperin**](https://www.eosworldwide.com/michael-halperin)

# Topic: Delegate and Elevate

### Advice and Insight for Entrepreneurs and Leadership Teams

[Clarity Break Thoughts](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break)

Search

### [Delegate and Elevate: How EOS Helps Leaders Stop Doing Everything Themselves](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/delegate-and-elevate-how-eos-helps-leaders-stop-doing-everything-themselves)

Many leaders reach a point where growth feels harder instead of easier. The business depends on them for decisions, approvals, problem-solving, and day-to-day execution. They are involved in everything, not

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/delegate-and-elevate-how-eos-helps-leaders-stop-doing-everything-themselves)

### [Stop Being the Bottleneck: Start Working on Your Business, Not Just in It](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/stop-being-the-bottleneck-start-working-on-your-business-not-just-in-it)

Let’s be honest, being a business owner can feel like death by a thousand interruptions. You’re the first one in, the last one out, and in between, you’re bouncing between

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/stop-being-the-bottleneck-start-working-on-your-business-not-just-in-it)

### [How Can We Solve “Busy”?](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/how-can-we-solve-busy)

Each of us can design our days, weeks, and months to better suit our time and talents. We must become more aware of the misused time we put on our calendars. That includes the events and projects we say “yes” to when we probably shouldn’t.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/how-can-we-solve-busy)

### [When EOS® Helps You Get Out of Business](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/eos-helps-business)

As a successful entrepreneur, Marc Levine spent countless hours thinking about his business, worrying about details large and small. All he ever wanted was to have a profitable business where employees liked working and customers wanted to patronize.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/eos-helps-business)

### [Doing Well to Do Good](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/doing-well-to-do-good)

As a multi-generation family business, change was hard. But all it took was a 90 Minute Meeting with an EOS Implementer®, to immediately realize how EOS could transform the trajectory of Blanton’s.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/doing-well-to-do-good)

### [EOS Worldwide Visionary Mark O’Donnell Offers Four Tactics Entrepreneurs Can Use to Master the Art of Delegation](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/mark-odonnell-forbes)

In his article, “Four Strategies For Clearing Your Plate To Make Room For Your True Passions,” Mark writes about how business leaders and entrepreneurs tend to get overworked and overwhelmed across the board. To solve the problem requires delegating.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/mark-odonnell-forbes)

# Topic: EOS Life

### Advice and Insight for Entrepreneurs and Leadership Teams

[Clarity Break Thoughts](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break)

Search

### [When EOS® Helps You Get Out of Business](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/eos-helps-business)

As a successful entrepreneur, Marc Levine spent countless hours thinking about his business, worrying about details large and small. All he ever wanted was to have a profitable business where employees liked working and customers wanted to patronize.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/eos-helps-business)

### [Doing Well to Do Good](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/doing-well-to-do-good)

As a multi-generation family business, change was hard. But all it took was a 90 Minute Meeting with an EOS Implementer®, to immediately realize how EOS could transform the trajectory of Blanton’s.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/running-on-eos/doing-well-to-do-good)

### [What Kind Of Life Do You Really Want?](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/what-kind-of-life-do-you-want)

We could have a life that’s kind of leaning back or a life that’s leaning forward. We could have a life that’s big and bold. We could have a little bitty life. We could have a life that’s kind of different. Do you know what kind of life you want as your ideal life?

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/what-kind-of-life-do-you-want)

### [The EOS Life®: Gifting The EOS Life to Your Employees](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/eos-life-to-your-employees)

What if, every quarter, your managers met with each of their direct reports and went through each of the five points of The EOS Life and helped them move the needle a little bit toward doing what they love, with people they love, making a huge difference, getting compensated appropriately, and having time for other passions?

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/eos-life-to-your-employees)

### [How to Measure Progress on Your EOS Life® Journey](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/measure-progress-eos-life)

It really is progress and not perfection. If you wait until you’re perfect and everything in your life is perfect, life will pass you by. Get started on this EOS Life journey and start reaping the rewards today.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/measure-progress-eos-life)

### [10 Disciplines for Managing and Maximizing Your Energy™](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/10-disciplines)

Here are the 10 disciplines for managing and maximizing your energy. Each discipline is fast, simple, powerful and fully customizable. They will help you expand, focus and manage your energy.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/10-disciplines)

- [EOS Mastery](https://www.eosworldwide.com/category/blog) [Strong in Six](https://www.eosworldwide.com/category/strong-in-six)

# Good Meeting, Bad Meeting

[![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/bb1066ba4e872e440b3e6f04e6b9163bcdbf2bbf1126aa1efcb21acd5a0b4f5c?s=96&d=mm&r=g)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/paul-detlefs)

- [Paul Detlefs](https://www.eosworldwide.com/paul-detlefs)

![Level 10 Meeting](https://www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/EOS_MasterBlog_Good_BadMeetingB2-1.jpg)

[A _Wall Street Journal_ column](https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10001424052702304192704577404434001058726) cited a survey in which 47% of respondents claimed “too many meetings” was the biggest time-waster in their companies — by far the largest response to anything on the list.

In my experience, the problem is not too many meetings, but too many **_bad_** meetings.

Meetings are essential because they are where leaders lead and where collaboration happens. Great companies have great meetings and use them to drive their business forward. Bad meetings, however, are much more common and are productivity killers.

So, what makes a meeting **_good_** rather than **_bad_**? Let’s start with what a bad meeting looks like.

It begins five to 15 minutes late which wastes valuable team-members time and sends a message to the rest of the company that lack of discipline is perfectly acceptable. Once the meeting starts, it consists of lots of discussion, tangents, and trips down rabbit holes that are fun but not especially productive. Few issues will be truly solved and few if any decisions will be made. The phrase “we’ve discussed this to death, we’d better move on” may be heard. And because few issues are really solved, it will probably create the need for **_more bad meetings_**.

We’re fond of saying: “If this sounds familiar, don’t worry. You’re not alone. It’s extremely common.” The great news is that there’s help.

## The EOS Level 10 Meeting™

Companies running on [EOS® (the Entrepreneurial Operating System®)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos) use an extremely effective weekly meeting format that we call the Level 10 Meeting (the name comes from the rating the team gives each meeting — always striving for a perfect 10). This is a weekly meeting that’s 90 minutes long.

### **This meeting follows five simple rules:**

1. It’s held on the same day each week
2. It’s held at the same time each week
3. Follows the same agenda
4. Starts on time
5. Ends on time

### **During a Level 10 Meeting, team members in a business running on EOS:**

1. Take the pulse of their business
2. Hold each other accountable for what they promised each other would be done by that meeting
3. Smoke out new issues
4. Prioritize and solve the issues that are most getting in the way right now

### **There are two other rules for the Level 10 Meeting:**

1. During the reporting part of the meeting, no discussion is allowed. Instead, the team drops anything that’s off track, including important issues (those will be handled during the issue-solving part of the meeting). This stops the team from going down rabbit holes early on.
2. [Issue-solving](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/dont-duct-tape-your-issues-solve-them) follows a known, proven process for prioritizing the most important issues, quickly getting to the root cause and then solving them, permanently. The result is that lots of issues get solved.

Every team I’ve helped implement EOS has reported that this meeting change was the first part of the system that they got value from. And every one of those teams reported that they started seeing that step-up in value within the first month of their EOS journey.

It’s no surprise. Team members walk out of their Level 10 Meetings better connected to each other because they all know what every other member of the team is working on and struggling with. This makes the team stronger and more cohesive. They also walk out with lots of issues and roadblocks removed. This frees them up to getting back in their lanes and making significant progress over the next week. And because they got so many issues solved at one time, it means they need fewer meetings, which enables them to get even more done.

If you’re having bad meetings, it’s time you gave the EOS Level 10 Meeting a try!

_Previously published on_ [_TheTractionGroup blog_](https://www.thetractiongroup.com/2019/07/08/good-meeting-bad-meeting/)

## Next Steps:

- Watch this [helpful video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1_iAxgB5k-k) on leading world-class meetings.
- Download a copy of our free eBook, [_How to Lead World-Class Meetings_](https://traction.eosworldwide.com/free-ebook-world-class-meetings)
- [Unlock the EOS Toolbox™](https://traction.eosworldwide.com/eos-toolbox) to download a copy of the Level 10 Meeting™ Agenda

[![Wasting Time In Inefficient Meetings](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/434170/53ee9546-6ec9-4d59-a76e-414313a3ef09.png)](https://cta-redirect.hubspot.com/cta/redirect/434170/53ee9546-6ec9-4d59-a76e-414313a3ef09)

[![Picture of Paul Detlefs](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/bb1066ba4e872e440b3e6f04e6b9163bcdbf2bbf1126aa1efcb21acd5a0b4f5c?s=300&d=mm&r=g)](https://www.eosworldwide.com/paul-detlefs)

[**Paul Detlefs**](https://www.eosworldwide.com/paul-detlefs)

# Topic: Processes

### Advice and Insight for Entrepreneurs and Leadership Teams

[Clarity Break Thoughts](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break)

Search

### [The Underestimated Advantage: The Power of Documented Processes](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-underestimated-advantage-the-power-of-documented-processes)

Freedom. It’s what every entrepreneur wants. More time, more control, more growth, more clarity. But without clearly documented processes, freedom stays just out of reach. You hire good people and

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-underestimated-advantage-the-power-of-documented-processes)

### [Reviewing Core Processes](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/reviewing-core-processes)

Having strong core processes for your business can save untold wasted hours trying to solve the wrong problems. As my clients prepare to roll out processes to their entire organization, they want to ensure they’re followed by all (FBA). To do that, I recommend the leadership team first review core processes together.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/reviewing-core-processes)

### [Are You Using a Broken  Ladder To Run Your Business?](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/broken-ladder-run-your-business)

If you struggle to identify and complete the core processes for your business, you’re not alone. Most entrepreneurs approach completing a process with the same amount of enthusiasm as a trip to the dentist for a root canal. However, you and your business benefit from well-defined processes that provide consistency. With 6 to 10 core processes documented and everyone in your organization following them to the letter, you can scale your business.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/broken-ladder-run-your-business)

### [Innovating to Achieve New Goals](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/innovating-achieve-new-goals)

In 1918, the Postal Service started experimenting with sending mail via the state-of-the-art aircraft of the time: the biplane. Transcontinental airmail was born. The U.S. Postal Service learned the importance of innovating to achieve new goals. You can do the same for your business.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/innovating-achieve-new-goals)

### [Top 10 Reasons to Hire an EOS® Implementer](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/10-reasons-to-hire-an-implementer)

The external objectivity, expertise, and ability an Implementer bring help you and your team get there faster.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/10-reasons-to-hire-an-implementer)

### [Getting Real with Your Accountability Chart™ in a Family Business](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/accountability-chart-in-family-business)

Completing the Accountability Chart can be very stressful, especially in a family business – once completed, it sets the foundation for success!

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/accountability-chart-in-family-business)

# Topic: Quarterly Conversations

### Advice and Insight for Entrepreneurs and Leadership Teams

[Clarity Break Thoughts](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break)

Search

### [Engage Your Employees With Your Vision](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/engage-employees-vision)

Three simple steps to find and create engaged, empowered employees who will buy into your company vision, stay longer, produce more efficiently and drive more profit to your bottom line.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/engage-employees-vision)

### [The Solution To Your Problems? Talking!](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/solution-problems-talking)

Effective communication is critical to the success of any business, especially when it comes to solving problems in your business.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/solution-problems-talking)

### [Trusting The Process](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/trusting-the-process)

Do you know what it takes not to just thrive, but to dominate your industry? Yes, I know that answer depends a lot on the industry. But I also know that following EOS purely and consistently, year after year, is what creates strong, enduring cultures that can adapt to the change and drive long-term growth.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/trusting-the-process)

### [The Quarterly Conversation – What’s Stopping You?](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break/the-quarterly-conversation-whats-stopping-you)

The quarterly conversation is simply a one-hour, one-on-one meeting with each of your direct reports every 90 days to discuss what’s working and what’s not.

After teaching it to our clients for more than 15 years, the feedback and results are undeniable. It’s high-impact, due to the limited time it requires (one hour per quarter, per direct report) and the outcomes it produces. The outcomes are issues getting solved, improved communication, faster growth, and a stronger and healthier bond between you and your direct report, just to name a few. The math is a no-brainer.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/clarity-break/the-quarterly-conversation-whats-stopping-you)

### [The Quarterly Conversation](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/594-eos-the-quarterly-conversation)

Are you having quarterly conversations with all of your direct reports? One of the most powerful things you can do is to stay on the same page with your people and help to make little course corrections along the way. Above and beyond your regular weekly meetings, day-to-day interactions and annual performance reviews, I recommend you follow this practice.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/594-eos-the-quarterly-conversation)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/benbella-books-01.png)](https://www.benbellabooks.com/shop/rocket-fuel/)

[![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/06/Logo2.png)](https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/rocket-fuel-gino-wickman/1120254230?ean=9781941631157#/)

## A POWERFUL COMBINATION TO FUEL YOUR BUSINESS

### ROCKET FUEL: THE ONE ESSENTIAL COMBINATION

Without an Integrator to turn a vision into reality, a Visionary is far less likely to succeed long-term and realize the company’s ultimate goals—likewise, without a Visionary, an Integrator can’t rise to his or her full potential. When these two people share their natural talents and innate skill sets, they have the power to reach new heights for virtually any company or organization.

_Rocket Fuel_ explains the critical roles of the Visionary and Integrator and how a relationship between the two can thrive. It can even help Visionaries and Integrators find one another. Rocket Fuel includes assessments to help you determine if you’re a Visionary or an Integrator so you can recognize your needs and find the best partner to suit them.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/OFFICIAL-Rocket-Fuel-Book-Stack-with-TL-Badge-1024x683.png)

### In this book you will learn:

- How to tell which one you are: Visionary or Integrator
- How a Visionary and Integrator work together

- How to find your perfect match

Download the free chapter in [EOS Academy.](https://academy.eosworldwide.com/c/books-the-traction-library/sections/847611/lessons/3220362)

## SAVE 50% ON BULK ORDERS OF ALL 20+ EOS BOOKS

#### The Traction Library offers a resource for every employee in your organization. Get your entire team on board and gain traction faster!

[Save 50% on Bulk Orders](https://www.eosworldwide.com/traction-library-bulk-order)

Contact Lindsay Marshall \| [lindsay@benbellabooks.com](mailto:lindsay@benbellabooks.com)

## Meet the Authors

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/gino-square.jpg)

### Gino Wickman

Gino Wickman is the author of the bestselling book, _Traction,_ and is passionate about helping people get what they want from their businesses. To fulfill that passion, Wickman created the [Entrepreneurial Operating System](https://www.eosworldwide.com/what-is-eos) ® (EOS), a holistic system that, when implemented in an organization, helps leaders run better businesses, get better control, have better life balance, and gain more traction—with the entire organization advancing together as a healthy, functional, and cohesive team. Wickman spends most of his time as an EOS Implementer, working hands-on with the leadership teams of entrepreneurial companies to help them fully implement EOS in their organizations.

### Mark C. Winters

Mark’s passion is helping entrepreneurs get unstuck so they can expand their freedom. Depending on the unique situation, Mark’s talent for introducing just the right combination of perspective and process sparks teams to start moving, move faster, or begin moving in the proper direction – with clarity. As a teacher, coach, and facilitator, Mark spends most of his time directly engaged with entrepreneurial leadership teams as a Certified EOS Implementer® — helping them implement EOS® in their own companies.

![](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/mark-winters2.jpg)

### [BY Lisa González](https://www.eosworldwide.com/lisa-gonzalez)

### [Why Doesn’t Anyone Follow Our Business Processes?](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/why-doesnt-anyone-follow-our-business-processes)

August 26, 2022

Don’t we have a process for closing the books? She keeps missing the monthly deadline. We shared these tools when we onboarded him; why isn’t he following the process? If you have heard or expressed frustrations similar to those above, you’re not alone. In our earlier blog, we shared the first tool to strengthen your Process Component™: The 3-Step Process Documenter™. Many organizations devote time and resources to this first step of documenting and simplifying their core processes, but don’t get the benefit of this work. This second and vital tool, the FBA Checklist, provides you with the four steps to get your core processes followed by all.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/why-doesnt-anyone-follow-our-business-processes)

### [Three-Steps to Meaningful Process Improvement](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/three-steps-to-meaningful-process-improvement)

August 22, 2022

Earlier in my career, I joined a residential remodel start-up that a family member had founded.  When we had only one location, the company thrived. As we expanded into other locations, however, we spent years struggling to consistently deliver our brand promise of projects that finished on time and on budget.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/three-steps-to-meaningful-process-improvement)

The start of a new year is when many of us make plans to eat better, move more, or finally follow through on personal goals. But it’s also one of

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/5-tips-to-set-rocks-that-dont-fall-flat-in-2026)

### [The Quarterly Paradigm: How a 90-Day WORLD Drives Sustained Growth](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-quarterly-paradigm-how-a-90-day-world-drives-sustained-growth)

You don’t need another traditional five-year plan full of guesses and spreadsheets. You need the next 90 days to matter. Most entrepreneurial leaders are wired for urgency. They move fast,

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-quarterly-paradigm-how-a-90-day-world-drives-sustained-growth)

### [What Does Done Look Like? How to Set SMART Rocks That Get Done](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/what-does-done-look-like-how-to-set-smart-rocks-that-get-done)

When gaining meaningful traction in your business, one EOS Tool turns ideas into action more consistently than any other: Rocks. Rocks are the big priorities you commit to completing within

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/what-does-done-look-like-how-to-set-smart-rocks-that-get-done)

### [Tips for Writing Great Rocks](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/tips-writing-smart-rocks)

Everyone who runs a company on EOS< struggles with how to set Rocks at the start of their journey. If you are having issues with setting your quarterly priorities, take heart – you are not alone! And it is not as difficult as it may seem at first.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/tips-writing-smart-rocks)

### [How to Master EOS® with 10,000 Punches](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/how-master-eos-10000-punches)

If you’ve just started your journey using EOS to run your business, you probably feel overwhelmed. Don’t worry! That’s normal. When you start anything new, it won’t come easily. It helps to remember that repetition, form, and coaching hold the keys to your success. Here are some tips on how to master EOS with 10,000 punches.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/how-master-eos-10000-punches)

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/how-eos-tools-define-quality)

![Diagram for evaluating the people you hire at your company: puppies, stars, rats, or enemies](https://www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Puppies_Stars.jpg)We’ve all heard the phrase “ [Hire slowly and Fire fast](https://blog.eosworldwide.com/blog/141616/eos/hire-slow-fire-fast/#axzz3xc7ZRRFB),” but most companies do the exact opposite. ### [The Issues You’re Normalizing Are Costing You More Than You Think](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-issues-youre-normalizing-are-costing-you-more-than-you-think)

April 1, 2026

You know that issue. The one that’s been on your Issues List so long it practically has seniority. It shows up in your Level 10 Meeting like clockwork. Someone mentions

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/the-issues-youre-normalizing-are-costing-you-more-than-you-think)

### [Four Steps to Solving Issues](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/four-steps-to-solving-issues)

August 8, 2022

When coaching a team to run on the Entrepreneurial Operating System® (EOS®), I love showing them how to actually solve their issues. At EOS, we say issues are problems, challenges, obstacles, opportunities, and new ideas worth your attention. Issues can be anything – good or bad – that your team may need to resolve. As part of the Level 10 Meeting™ agenda, leaders Identify, Discuss, and Solve (IDS™) business issues for the majority of the meeting. Below I’ll break down the four steps to solving issues forever.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/four-steps-to-solving-issues)

### [Help Them See What You’re Saying](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/help-them-see-what-youre-saying)

February 7, 2020

Vision. I went to the eye doctor and for the first time, I couldn’t read anything below the top line. Really?!! And how many other reminders do I need that

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/help-them-see-what-youre-saying)

### [When Your Employee Isn’t A Fit](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/employee-does-not-fit)

January 23, 2020

![when an employee isn't a fit](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/single-tree-in-desert.jpg)As a Minnesota resident, I often find myself dreaming of warm climates this time of year.

The temperature hovers around freezing and I can picture the arid deserts of Arizona or the lush rainforests of Hawaii. Both climates offer respite from the Minnesota winter, but they are decidedly different from one another in flora and fauna.

A cactus can’t survive in the rainforest, and ferns can’t grow in the desert.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/employee-does-not-fit)

### [Where You Go, They Go](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/where-you-go-they-go)

June 28, 2019

![creating company culture ](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/art-background-brick-272254.jpg)Company culture. Many people talk about, but not everyone puts in the effort to create it. And when they don’t, it becomes assumed – which isn’t what you want.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/where-you-go-they-go)

### [Keep Your Business on Track](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/keep-business-focused-on-track)

March 2, 2019

## Keep Your Eyes on the Road: Using Your Core Focus to Keep Your Business on Track

![EOS Core Focus Keep Your Business on Track](https://www.eosworldwide.com/nitropack_static/qgYiUxkzTzUOyXHSfAsrqggrSjYfCRzB/assets/images/optimized/rev-2541913/www.eosworldwide.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/EOS%20Core%20Focus%20Keep%20Your%20Business%20on%20Track.jpg)Imagine driving on a highway you’ve never been on before. Preferably you’re driving a convertible and the views are breathtaking. In this scenario, it’s unlikely you’ll notice the guardrails. However, the guardrails are almost invisibly keeping you on track, guiding you safely and efficiently to your desired destination. In your business, your Core Focus™️ functions the same way.

[Read More »](https://www.eosworldwide.com/blog/keep-business-focused-on-track)
